explanationZhTw: gelatinous 原意是「凝膠狀的、果凍般的」；這裡是比喻，表示他累到全身軟爛、毫無力氣。
generatedExample: After the overnight shift, he felt gelatinous with fatigue.

explanationZhTw: spontaneously 指「自然而然地、沒有預先計畫地」，表示念頭是突然冒出來的。
generatedExample: The idea came to her spontaneously while she was walking home.

explanationZhTw: translucency 是「半透明」的狀態，表示能透光但不是完全透明。
generatedExample: The translucency of the paper made the lamp glow softly.

explanationZhTw: drained out of him 這裡是慣用說法，表示「被徹底耗盡、像被抽乾一樣」。
generatedExample: The long meeting drained all the energy out of him.

explanationZhTw: debauch 原本多指「放縱、縱欲」；這裡是文學性的反常搭配，表示極度過度、失控的工作消耗。
generatedExample: He treated the deadline week as a debauch of overtime and caffeine.

explanationZhTw: magnified 這裡不是單純「放大」，而是指感覺被強化、變得特別明顯。
generatedExample: In the quiet room, every small noise seemed magnified.

explanationZhTw: fretted 這裡表示「摩擦、磨痛、使人不舒服」，不是一般的「煩擾」。
generatedExample: The stiff collar fretted his neck all day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用語，表示「結束了、完了」。
generatedExample: After the final exam, school was all over for the summer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是強調字面意思，而是用來加強語氣，表示「真的、完全地」。
generatedExample: I was literally the only person in the office.

explanationZhTw: 指「藏身處、躲藏的地方」。
generatedExample: They found a hiding-place behind the old bookshelf.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「走上去、沿著往前走」。
generatedExample: We walked up the street to the train station.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示「保持警覺、提防著」。
generatedExample: I slept with one eye open because the hotel felt unsafe.

explanationZhTw: 指「公事包、手提文件箱」。
generatedExample: He put the papers into his brief-case before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「在他手上、由他持有」，重點是擁有或掌控，而不是抽象的「所有權」本身。
generatedExample: The museum kept the painting in its possession for many years.

explanationZhTw: 「processions」指遊行隊伍或列隊前進的儀式性行進。這裡強調集體宣傳活動的排場與規模。
generatedExample: The town held processions to celebrate the festival.

explanationZhTw: 「banners」是橫幅、旗幟或標語布條。此處是群眾運動中常見的政治宣傳物件。
generatedExample: The students carried banners across the campus.

explanationZhTw: 「waxworks」字面是蠟像，這裡是用來展示、供人觀看的人形模型或假人。屬於帶有歷史感的具體名詞。
generatedExample: The museum displayed waxworks of famous actors.

explanationZhTw: 「caterpillars」原指毛蟲；在這裡是坦克履帶的比喻說法。屬於上下文中不常見的意義。
generatedExample: The caterpillars of the tank left deep tracks in the mud.

explanationZhTw: 「massed」在這裡不是一般的「聚集的」而是形容大量編隊、成群出現的，帶有軍事或宏大場面的意味。
generatedExample: The sky filled with the massed birds moving south.

explanationZhTw: 「climax」是高潮、最緊張或最強烈的時刻。這裡描述情緒與聲勢達到頂點。
generatedExample: The concert reached its climax during the final song.

explanationZhTw: 「boiled up」是片語動詞，這裡不是「煮沸」，而是指情緒或仇恨「高漲、湧上來」。
generatedExample: Her anger boiled up after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 「delirium」在此不是醫學上單純的譫妄，而是形容群眾陷入近乎失控、狂亂的狀態。
generatedExample: The crowd was in a delirium after the victory.

explanationZhTw: 「publicly hanged」表示「公開絞死、當眾處決」。這裡是強烈的政治暴力語境。
generatedExample: The prisoner was publicly hanged in the square.

explanationZhTw: 「proceedings」是較正式的「程序、活動流程」，這裡指整個公開活動或事件的進行過程。
generatedExample: The proceedings began after everyone took their seats.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `admission` 不是「入場」，而是「承認、供認」。整句表示官方沒有承認有任何改變發生。
generatedExample: There was no admission that the policy had failed.

explanationZhTw: `take part in` 表示「參與、加入」。這裡指溫斯頓正在參加示威活動。
generatedExample: She took part in the school debate.

explanationZhTw: `demonstration` 在這裡是「示威、抗議活動」，不是單純的「示範」。
generatedExample: Thousands joined a demonstration in the city center.

explanationZhTw: `floodlit` 指被強光照亮；`luridly` 在這裡強調那種刺眼、陰森的光線效果。整體是偏文學性的描述。
generatedExample: The stadium was floodlit for the evening match.

explanationZhTw: `packed with` 表示「擠滿、塞滿」。這裡是說廣場上人很多。
generatedExample: The train was packed with commuters.

explanationZhTw: `harangue` 表示「對某人激動地長篇訓斥或煽動性演說」。這裡比單純的 `speak` 更強烈，帶有咄咄逼人的意味。
generatedExample: The politician spent an hour haranguing the audience.

explanationZhTw: `boom forth` 表示「洪亮地發出、轟然響起」。這裡用來形容擴音後的聲音非常響亮、有壓迫感。
generatedExample: The announcer's voice boomed forth across the hall.

explanationZhTw: `boil over` 原意是「煮到溢出」，在這裡比喻情緒或怒氣「爆發、失控」。
generatedExample: Tension boiled over during the final minutes of the game.

explanationZhTw: `drown` 在這裡不是溺水，而是指「被淹沒、被蓋過」。演講者的聲音被群眾的吼叫蓋掉了。
generatedExample: Her words were drowned by the noise from the street.

explanationZhTw: `be at work` 這裡表示「在活動中、正在做某事」，常帶有某種力量或陰謀正在運作的意思。
generatedExample: Something strange was at work behind the scenes.

explanationZhTw: `rip from` 表示「猛地撕下、扯下」。這裡形容海報被暴力地從牆上撕下。
generatedExample: They ripped the old posters from the walls.

explanationZhTw: `go straight on with` 表示「完全沒有停頓，直接繼續做」。這裡強調演說者對突發事件毫不受影響。
generatedExample: She went straight on with her presentation after the interruption.

explanationZhTw: 「looking back」在這裡是「回顧／事後看來」的意思，表示從事後角度回想過去。
generatedExample: Looking back, I can see why that decision was hard to make.

explanationZhTw: 「preoccupy」指「使某人掛心、分心」，重點是某事佔據了他的注意力。
generatedExample: The new problem began to preoccupy her more than she expected.

explanationZhTw: 「torn down」是片語動詞，這裡表示海報被撕掉、拆除或移除。
generatedExample: The old banners were torn down after the event ended.

explanationZhTw: 「abstractedly」表示「心不在焉地、恍神地」，動作做了但注意力不在眼前的事情上。
generatedExample: She answered abstractedly while thinking about her train schedule.

explanationZhTw: 「opportunity」是常見單字，意思是「機會、時機」。
generatedExample: I waited for an opportunity to ask my question.

explanationZhTw: 「likewise」表示「同樣地、也一樣」，用來說前面提到的動作別人也做了。
generatedExample: I cleaned my desk, and my teammate did likewise.

explanationZhTw: 「issuing from」在這裡不是「發行」，而是「從……發出、流出、傳出」的意思。
generatedExample: Warm air was issuing from the vents above the door.

explanationZhTw: 「recalling them to their posts」在這裡是「召回他們回到崗位」的意思，屬於較正式的語境用法。
generatedExample: The loudspeaker was recalling the guards to their posts.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「過時、失效」的意思，指政治資料因局勢改變而不再可用。
generatedExample: The old policy memo is obsolete after the new law passed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「修好」，而是指把內容迅速改成符合最新官方版本，也就是更正、改寫。
generatedExample: The archive entries were rectified before the report went public.

explanationZhTw: 正式命令、指示，常見於政府或組織語境。
generatedExample: We received a directive to delete the outdated files.

explanationZhTw: 同盟；在政治語境中指雙方建立合作關係。
generatedExample: The two countries signed an alliance for mutual defense.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示工作多到讓人應付不過來，重點是壓倒性負擔，不只是「很大」。
generatedExample: The amount of mail was overwhelming by noon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「零碎、短暫的小睡」的意思。
generatedExample: I only got snatches of sleep during the night shift.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示中斷、停下正在做的事。
generatedExample: She broke off to answer the phone.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指可用說話來記錄文字的裝置。
generatedExample: He dictated the memo into the speakwrite.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「絕非、完全不」。
generatedExample: The plan is by no means perfect, but it will work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 substitute 是「以一個取代另一個」；在這句中指把名字換掉。
generatedExample: We can substitute lemon for lime in this recipe.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「搬移物品」，而是把戰爭的地點或敘述從一個區域改到另一個區域。
generatedExample: They spent hours transferring the files to a new server.

explanationZhTw: 眼鏡；這裡是較正式、偏舊式的說法。
generatedExample: She cleaned her spectacles before reading the note.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 crushing 不是「壓碎」字面義，而是指「極其艱鉅、令人喘不過氣」的。
generatedExample: After ten hours of lifting boxes, even opening the door felt like a crushing physical task.

explanationZhTw: 偽造品；常指假文件、假簽名或仿冒作品。
generatedExample: The police discovered that the signature on the contract was a forgery.

explanationZhTw: dribble 在這裡不是「流口水」，而是指少量、零星地慢慢流出來。
generatedExample: The dribble of emails from the client stopped after lunchtime.

explanationZhTw: 逐漸減緩、變少；這是常見的片語動詞。
generatedExample: The rain eased off just before sunset.

explanationZhTw: 某種程度上說、可以說；用來讓說法聽起來比較委婉。
generatedExample: He was, as it were, the unofficial leader of the group.

explanationZhTw: 溫溫的、微溫的；常用來形容水或飲料不夠熱也不夠冷。
generatedExample: The tea was tepid, so I asked for a fresh cup.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `voluptuous` 不是指「豐滿的」外表，而是形容聲音或動作帶有一種濃烈、感官上很強的質感，接近「柔靡的、肉感的、帶點感官享受的」意思。
generatedExample: The old door opened with a voluptuous groan.

explanationZhTw: `presently` 在這裡表示「不久後、等一下就」，是較文學或正式的用法，不是「目前」的意思。
generatedExample: The doctor will see you presently.

explanationZhTw: `sluttish` 這裡不是指道德上的意思，而是形容東西「骯髒的、邋遢的、破舊不整潔的」。
generatedExample: The sluttish room was full of dust and old newspapers.

explanationZhTw: `brief-case` 是「公事包、文件包」，指上班或攜帶文件用的手提箱包。
generatedExample: He carried the contracts in a battered brief-case.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「書冊、卷」，不是一般的「容量」或「音量」。
generatedExample: He opened the old volume and found a letter inside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 bound 不是「綁住」，而是指書本「裝訂成冊、封面裝訂」。
generatedExample: The notebook was bound in leather.

explanationZhTw: 表示頁緣因長期使用而磨損、破舊。
generatedExample: The old map was worn at the edges.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「散開、碎裂、變得支離破碎」。
generatedExample: My backpack fell apart after years of use.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示這本書曾經被很多不同的人拿過、用過。
generatedExample: This diary has passed through many hands over the decades.

explanationZhTw: 指書的「扉頁／題名頁」，也就是印有書名與出版資訊的那一頁。
generatedExample: The author signed the title-page of the first edition.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 ran 不是「跑」，而是表示題字或內容「寫著、是這樣說的」。
generatedExample: The notice ran: 'Keep off the grass.'

explanationZhTw: 「理論」；指用來解釋事物或指導行動的原則、想法。
generatedExample: Her theory was that the delay came from a software bug.

explanationZhTw: 「實踐／做法」；指把概念或規則實際應用到現實中的行動。
generatedExample: In practice, the new rule was hard to follow.

explanationZhTw: 「寡頭統治的」；描述由少數人掌權的政治結構。
generatedExample: The novel describes an oligarchical regime that controls every public message.

explanationZhTw: 「集體主義」；強調群體利益高於個人利益的思想。
generatedExample: The movement promoted collectivism over individual ownership.

explanationZhTw: 章節；這裡是書中的篇章標題，用羅馬數字 I 表示第 1 章。
generatedExample: Chapter 2 starts with a new character.

explanationZhTw: 無知；在這裡是政治口號的一部分，意思不是單純指「不知道」，而是帶有諷刺和控制思想的語氣。
generatedExample: Ignoring the facts can lead to ignorance.

explanationZhTw: 力量；在這句口號裡，這個字被用來表示「權力、控制或精神上的強大」，帶有特定政治語境。
generatedExample: Teamwork gave the group real strength.

explanationZhTw: 「recorded time」在這裡不是指錄音或錄影的時間，而是指有文字或史料可考的歷史時期。
generatedExample: Throughout recorded time, people have tried to explain why societies rise and fall.

explanationZhTw: 「subdivided」是「再細分、細分成更小部分」的意思。
generatedExample: The class was subdivided into smaller groups for the project.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「relative numbers」是指「相對數量、比例」，不是單純的數學關係。
generatedExample: The relative numbers of volunteers and staff changed after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「altered」表示「改變、變動」，這裡強調社會的基本結構始終沒有變。
generatedExample: The plan was altered after the first meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「upheavals」指劇烈動盪、翻天覆地的變化，常用來描述社會或政治局勢大變。
generatedExample: The country went through political upheavals before the election.

explanationZhTw: 「irrevocable changes」是「不可逆轉的改變」，表示一旦發生就無法回到原狀。
generatedExample: The decision caused irrevocable changes in the company.

explanationZhTw: 「reasserted itself」意思是某種模式、力量或趨勢再次變強並重新顯現。
generatedExample: After a short pause, the old habit reasserted itself.

explanationZhTw: 「equilibrium」是「平衡、均勢」；這裡用物理概念比喻社會最後會回到穩定狀態。
generatedExample: The market slowly returned to equilibrium after the shock.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「目標、目的」；不是動詞 aim「瞄準」，而是名詞用法，表示各群體想達成的事情。
generatedExample: The aims of the two teams are very different.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「群體、團體」；泛指有共同立場或利益的一群人。
generatedExample: Several groups met to discuss the plan.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是副詞，表示「完全地、徹底地」，用來強調後面的形容詞。
generatedExample: I entirely agree with your idea.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是較正式且具文學感的用法，意思是「無法調和的、彼此衝突到不能妥協的」。
generatedExample: Their positions on the issue were irreconcilable.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「主要地、特別地」；不是字面上的其他用法。
generatedExample: She called chiefly to confirm the meeting time.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的虛構裝置，兼具螢幕與監視功能，用來播放資訊並監控人。
generatedExample: The telescreen stayed on all night, so he never felt fully alone.

explanationZhTw: 指「回頭瞥一眼」，帶出提防、緊張的感覺。
generatedExample: She glanced over her shoulder before opening the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 play 不是「玩」，而是指輕拂、吹拂到臉上。
generatedExample: A cool breeze played against the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示聲音「飄來、傳來」，不是指物體漂浮在水面。
generatedExample: Music floated down from the apartment above.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻，指時鐘聲像昆蟲般細小、嗡嗡作響，強調房間很安靜。
generatedExample: The old heater gave the room an insect voice at night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 settle into 表示「舒服地安頓下來、坐得更放鬆」。
generatedExample: He settled into the sofa with a blanket and a book.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「把腳抬高放鬆」，常指休息、偷閒。
generatedExample: After work, I like to put my feet up for half an hour.

explanationZhTw: 指「極度幸福、無比愉快」的狀態，語氣很強。
generatedExample: Lying in the sun with no deadlines felt like bliss.

explanationZhTw: 表示「最終、終究」；在這裡強調遲早會讀完又重讀。
generatedExample: Ultimately, the team chose the simpler design.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「戰爭就是和平」，而是《1984》中的極權口號。它刻意用矛盾的說法來顛倒常識，讓人接受政權的宣傳與控制。
generatedExample: In the poster, the slogan "War is Peace" reminds everyone how the regime twists language to control thought.

explanationZhTw: 「splitting up」在這裡表示把某個整體分成幾個部分，意思接近「分裂、劃分成幾塊」。
generatedExample: The company is splitting up into three separate businesses.

explanationZhTw: 「foreseen」表示「預見、預先想到」。這裡是說這種局勢在當時之前就已經可以被預測到。
generatedExample: The delay was foreseen by the project team.

explanationZhTw: 「absorption」這裡不是「吸收」飲食那種意思，而是指一個地區或勢力被另一個勢力吞併、納入版圖。
generatedExample: The absorption of the small kingdom by its neighbor changed the map.

explanationZhTw: 「emerged」表示「出現、成形」。在這裡是說東亞這個第三勢力是在之後才明確形成。
generatedExample: A new pattern emerged during the experiment.

explanationZhTw: 「arbitrary」表示「任意的、沒有固定或明顯規則的」。這裡是說有些邊界不是自然形成，而是看起來很隨意。
generatedExample: The deadline felt arbitrary because no one explained how it was chosen.

explanationZhTw: 「fluctuate」表示「上下波動、時常變動」。這裡是說邊界會隨著戰爭局勢改變。
generatedExample: Prices fluctuate depending on demand.

explanationZhTw: 「fortunes of war」是習語，指「戰爭局勢的變化、戰爭中的勝負與運氣」。
generatedExample: The city changed hands several times, depending on the fortunes of war.

explanationZhTw: 「comprises」表示「包含、由……組成」。這裡是在界定這個地區包含哪些範圍。
generatedExample: The class comprises students from five different countries.

explanationZhTw: 「definite」在這裡表示「明確的、固定的」。它不是單純「肯定的」，而是指邊界不夠清楚。
generatedExample: We still do not have a definite answer.

explanationZhTw: 「portion」表示「一部分、片段」。這裡是說其中一部分地區的歸屬會變動。
generatedExample: A large portion of the report focuses on costs.

explanationZhTw: 「super-states」指超級強權／超級國家，帶有虛構政治體的意味，不是一般的「國家」。
generatedExample: The novel is set in a world controlled by three super-states.

explanationZhTw: 「at war」表示「交戰中、處於戰爭狀態」。這裡是固定片語。
generatedExample: The two countries have been at war for years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「annihilating」不是單純的破壞，而是「徹底毀滅、幾乎全滅」的意思。
generatedExample: The blast caused an annihilating level of destruction.

explanationZhTw: 「combatants」是正式用語，指交戰雙方、戰鬥人員。
generatedExample: The combatants agreed to meet after the ceasefire.

explanationZhTw: 「bloodthirsty」指嗜血的、殘暴好戰的，強調對暴力或殺戮的偏好。
generatedExample: The dictator was portrayed as a bloodthirsty ruler.

explanationZhTw: 「chivalrous」原指騎士精神的；此處帶有「有風度、講榮譽」的意思。
generatedExample: He was chivalrous enough to let her finish speaking.

explanationZhTw: 「war hysteria」指戰爭恐慌、戰爭狂熱，通常表示集體性的極端情緒與失控氛圍。
generatedExample: War hysteria spread after the border attack.

explanationZhTw: 「looked upon as」是片語動詞／固定片語，意思是「被視為」。
generatedExample: The proposal was looked upon as a compromise.

explanationZhTw: 「meritorious」指有功的、值得嘉許的；此處是諷刺地說暴行在某些立場下被當成「有功」。
generatedExample: Her meritorious work earned her a promotion.

explanationZhTw: 「casualties」指傷亡人數，尤其是戰爭、事故中的死傷者。
generatedExample: The accident caused only a few casualties.

explanationZhTw: 「guess at」表示「猜測、勉強估計」，帶有不確定的意思。
generatedExample: We can only guess at the final cost for now.

explanationZhTw: 「consumption goods」指消費品、日用品；這裡是較正式的經濟用語。
generatedExample: Factories slowed down when imports of consumption goods fell.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「waged」不是一般「工資」相關用法，而是指「發動、進行（戰爭、運動、鬥爭）」。
generatedExample: The company waged a long campaign to rebuild trust.

explanationZhTw: 「in spite of」表示「儘管、雖然」，用來引出和前文相反或不受影響的情況。
generatedExample: In spite of the delay, we finished the work on time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「decisive」是「能決定勝負的、決定性的」，不是一般常見的「果斷」。
generatedExample: The last match was decisive for the championship.

explanationZhTw: 「definitively」在這裡表示「徹底地、完全地」，強調無法被真正消滅或最終解決。
generatedExample: The problem has not been definitively solved yet.

explanationZhTw: 「evenly matched」表示雙方或多方「勢均力敵、實力相當」。
generatedExample: The two players were evenly matched from the start.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「formidable」是「強大到令人畏懼的、很難對付的」。
generatedExample: She is a formidable opponent in any debate.

explanationZhTw: 「self-contained economies」指「自給自足、內部能運作的經濟體系」，重點是對外依賴較少。
generatedExample: The island developed a more self-contained economy over time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「geared to」是「使...互相配合、調整到能彼此運作」的意思。
generatedExample: The timetable is geared to commuters who start work early.

explanationZhTw: 「scramble for markets」表示「為了市場而激烈爭奪」，帶有競爭很急、很亂的感覺。
generatedExample: There was a scramble for markets in the new region.

explanationZhTw: 「come to an end」表示「結束、終止」。
generatedExample: The meeting finally came to an end at midnight.

explanationZhTw: 「a matter of life and death」是慣用語，表示「生死攸關、非常緊急或重要」。
generatedExample: For the patient, the timing of the surgery was a matter of life and death.

explanationZhTw: 「In so far as」表示「就...而言、在...範圍內」，用來限定說法的適用範圍。
generatedExample: In so far as I know, the report is accurate.

explanationZhTw: 「quadrilateral」是幾何或正式用語，指「四邊形」；這裡用來描述一塊大致四角形的區域。
generatedExample: The map marked a rough quadrilateral around the city center.

explanationZhTw: 「thickly-populated」表示「人口稠密的、人口很多的」。
generatedExample: They moved to a thickly-populated district near the station.

explanationZhTw: 「changing hands」是片語動詞，表示「易手、轉到別人手中」，常用於地盤、財產、權力等。
generatedExample: The old house has changed hands several times.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「stroke」不是「一擊」而是帶有策略意味的「一下子、一次行動」；整個片語指「一次背叛式的突襲行動」。
generatedExample: A single stroke of treachery changed the outcome of the war.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「dictates」不是單純的「口述」，而是「決定、支配、左右」的意思。
generatedExample: Practical needs often dictate the final design.

explanationZhTw: 有價值的礦物。
generatedExample: The region is known for its valuable minerals.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「產出、出產」的意思，不是一般的「屈服」。
generatedExample: These farms yield a lot of fruit in summer.

explanationZhTw: 合成；人工製造。
generatedExample: Scientists can synthesize the compound in a laboratory.

explanationZhTw: 形容幾乎取之不盡、用之不竭的儲備，是誇張說法。
generatedExample: The old library felt like a bottomless reserve of stories.

explanationZhTw: 廉價勞動力。
generatedExample: The company moved production overseas to find cheap labour.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「處理掉」，而是「支配、掌控」的意思。
generatedExample: Whoever holds the territory disposes also of its resources.

explanationZhTw: 把某人降低到奴隸地位；這裡強調被壓迫和失去自由。
generatedExample: The workers were reduced to the status of slaves by the regime.

explanationZhTw: 不斷在征服者之間轉手；表示所有權或控制權一再改變。
generatedExample: The city passed from conqueror to conqueror during the war.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「被消耗掉、被當作資源用完」的意思。
generatedExample: Their energy was expended on endless meetings.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「大量生產、製造」；不是「關掉後再出去」。
generatedExample: The factory can turn out thousands of phones a day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「大量生產、製造」；不是「關掉後再出去」。
generatedExample: The factory can turn out thousands of phones a day.

explanationZhTw: 來回變動；表示邊界或勢力範圍反覆移動。
generatedExample: The border seemed to flow back and forth for years.

explanationZhTw: 聲稱擁有；主張對某地或某物有權利。
generatedExample: Several countries lay claim to the same island.

explanationZhTw: 勢力均衡；力量大致相等、彼此制衡的狀態。
generatedExample: The treaty changed the balance of power in the region.

explanationZhTw: 核心地帶；中心區域。
generatedExample: The capital sits in the heartland of the country.

explanationZhTw: 未受侵犯的；完整無損的。
generatedExample: The ancient site remained inviolate for centuries.

explanationZhTw: 「primary aim」是「主要目的、首要目標」；這裡在說明戰爭真正要達成的核心功能。
generatedExample: The primary aim of the workshop is to help new employees understand the system.

explanationZhTw: 「warfare」是「戰爭、作戰」；比單純的 `war` 更偏向戰爭行為或軍事衝突整體。
generatedExample: Modern warfare often relies on technology as much as on soldiers.

explanationZhTw: 「DOUBLETHINK」是小說中的特定概念，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的想法，卻都不覺得矛盾。
generatedExample: The regime depended on doublethink to make citizens accept contradictions.

explanationZhTw: 「surplus」是「過剩、剩餘」；整個片語指「消費品的過剩」，也就是生產多於需求的商品。
generatedExample: After the harvest, the surplus of consumption goods was stored for winter.

explanationZhTw: 「latent」是「潛在的、尚未顯現的」；表示問題一直存在，只是還沒變得明顯。
generatedExample: There was a latent conflict between the two teams that later became obvious.

explanationZhTw: 「dilapidated」是「破敗的、失修的」；常用來形容建築或環境很舊、很糟。
generatedExample: They moved into a dilapidated house that needed major repairs.

explanationZhTw: 「imaginary」這裡不是指「想像力很強」，而是「想像中的、並不存在於現實的」。
generatedExample: The novel describes an imaginary future where cities float above the sea.

explanationZhTw: 「prodigious」表示「驚人的、非常大的」；這裡修飾 `speed`，強調發展速度極快。
generatedExample: The company grew at a prodigious speed after launching its app.

explanationZhTw: 「impoverishment」是「貧困化、使變窮」；這裡指戰爭與革命造成社會資源和生活水準下降。
generatedExample: The region suffered impoverishment after years of conflict.

explanationZhTw: 「regimented」是「嚴格管制的、軍事化管理的」；在這裡指社會被高度控制，缺乏自由。
generatedExample: Creativity is hard to maintain in a regimented workplace.

explanationZhTw: 「drudgery」是「單調乏味的苦工、苦差事」；通常指重複又辛苦、沒有樂趣的工作。
generatedExample: He left the job because he was tired of the drudgery.

explanationZhTw: 「living standards」是「生活水準」；指一個人或社會可享有的物質生活水平。
generatedExample: Higher wages can improve living standards for many families.

explanationZhTw: 財富；此處指可用的金錢、資產與生活資源。
generatedExample: Wealth can improve daily life, but it does not automatically create fairness.

explanationZhTw: 階層式社會；人群依權力或地位分層。
generatedExample: A hierarchical society often makes social mobility difficult.

explanationZhTw: 不再帶來特殊地位；這裡的 confer 是「賦予」的意思。
generatedExample: A title may confer no distinction if everyone has the same title.

explanationZhTw: 特權階層；受到制度保護、地位較高的一群人。
generatedExample: The board became a privileged caste that made decisions for everyone else.

explanationZhTw: 實際上；和理論或想像相對。
generatedExample: In practice, the new rule is harder to follow than it sounds.

explanationZhTw: 被貧窮麻痺得遲鈍；stupefied 表示使人頭腦不清、反應變慢。
generatedExample: After years of hunger and stress, many people were stupefied by poverty and fear.

explanationZhTw: 把它徹底推翻／清除；這裡是慣用的「sweep away」。
generatedExample: Public anger grew until people swept the old law away.

explanationZhTw: 從長遠來看；看整體後果而不是短期。
generatedExample: In the long run, careful planning saves time and money.

explanationZhTw: 可行的解決辦法；practicable 強調實際上做得到。
generatedExample: We need a practicable solution that works with the budget we have.

explanationZhTw: 機械化；用機器取代人工或提高自動化程度。
generatedExample: Mechanization changed farming and reduced the need for manual labor.

explanationZhTw: 近乎本能的；quasi-表示「近似、半像」。
generatedExample: Her quasi-instinctive reaction was to protect the child.

explanationZhTw: 工業上落後的；指工業發展程度較低。
generatedExample: The region stayed industrially backward for many years.

explanationZhTw: 被支配／受控制；這裡指在政治或軍事上處於弱勢。
generatedExample: Small countries can be dominated by larger powers.

explanationZhTw: 「output of goods」指商品的產出量或生產量；這裡是在說把物資產量限制住。
generatedExample: The factory increased its output of goods last month.

explanationZhTw: 「capitalism」是「資本主義」；這裡指一種以私有資本和市場交易為主的經濟制度。
generatedExample: Many countries adopted capitalism after the war.

explanationZhTw: 「stagnate」在這裡不是指水停滯，而是指經濟或發展「停滯不前」。
generatedExample: Without investment, the local economy began to stagnate.

explanationZhTw: 「went out of cultivation」指土地不再耕種、變成荒廢；這裡是說農地被閒置了。
generatedExample: After the village was abandoned, several fields went out of cultivation.

explanationZhTw: 「capital equipment」指資本設備，例如機器、工廠、工具等生產用資產。
generatedExample: The company invested in new capital equipment to raise productivity.

explanationZhTw: 「kept half alive」是帶有誇張色彩的說法，表示只能勉強維持生命、生活極度艱困。
generatedExample: The prisoners were kept half alive by tiny rations.

explanationZhTw: 「entailed」表示「導致、伴隨著、必然帶來」；這裡是說這種做法會造成軍事上的弱勢。
generatedExample: The new policy entailed extra costs for the company.

explanationZhTw: 「privations」指匱乏、苦難，尤其是缺乏食物、金錢或基本生活資源。
generatedExample: The refugees endured years of privations.

explanationZhTw: 「the wheels of industry turning」是比喻說法，意思是讓工業機器持續運作、讓經濟持續跑下去。
generatedExample: The loan helped keep the wheels of industry turning during the crisis.

explanationZhTw: 「continuous warfare」指持續不斷的戰爭狀態；這裡是說要靠長期戰爭來達成前述目的。
generatedExample: The region suffered from continuous warfare for decades.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「勞動」或「勞力」所產出的成果，不是日常口語裡單純的工作時間。
generatedExample: The new machine reduces the amount of labour needed on the farm.

explanationZhTw: 指「長遠來看、從長期角度看」。
generatedExample: In the long run, regular practice matters more than talent alone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「消耗、花費」的意思，特別是消耗勞動力或資源。
generatedExample: The project ended up expending a lot of time and money.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「吃掉」，而是「被使用、被消耗」。
generatedExample: Most of the fuel is consumed during the first hour of the flight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「報廢、丟棄不用」，不是日常口語中的「廢料」。
generatedExample: The old computer was scrapped after the company upgraded its system.

explanationZhTw: 指「過時的、已不再適用的」。
generatedExample: Some obsolete rules should be updated or removed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「大量消耗、吞掉」資源。
generatedExample: Rent and bills can eat up most of your monthly salary.

explanationZhTw: 指「剩餘、盈餘」，也就是超過必要需求的部分。
generatedExample: The bakery donated its surplus bread to a shelter.

explanationZhTw: 指「長期性的短缺」，強調問題持續存在而不是偶發。
generatedExample: The town has a chronic shortage of affordable housing.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「被視為、被看作」。
generatedExample: The delay was looked on as a sign that the plan needed work.

explanationZhTw: 「brink」是邊緣、臨界點；整體表示「幾乎要陷入困苦」。
generatedExample: After losing his job, he was on the brink of hardship.

explanationZhTw: 指「簡樸、克制、沒有奢華」的生活方式。
generatedExample: She chose an austere lifestyle with very few possessions.

explanationZhTw: 指「設備齊全、布置講究」的，帶有較高品質與舒適感。
generatedExample: They stayed in a well-appointed room with a view of the lake.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「沉沒」，而是指被壓在社會底層、看不見的群眾。
generatedExample: The policy was meant to help the submerged masses, not only the wealthy.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的特定詞彙，指「無產階級／平民大眾」，帶有階級區分意味。
generatedExample: In the novel, the proles live outside the main circles of power.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，表示整個社會像被包圍的城市一樣，充滿壓迫與匱乏感。
generatedExample: During the crisis, the office felt like a besieged city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是具體的比喻，表示連一小塊稀少的食物都可能決定生存差距。
generatedExample: In the emergency, a lump of horseflesh would have been valuable.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「把權力交出去、移交」；是帶有動作感的片語用法。
generatedExample: The handing-over of the keys took only a minute.

explanationZhTw: 指「階層、封閉的社會階級群體」，強調權力集中在少數人手中。
generatedExample: A small caste of officials controlled every decision.

explanationZhTw: `surplus labour` 指「多餘的勞動力／過剩工時」，這裡不是一般的「多餘」而是指超出社會真正需要的工作量。
generatedExample: The factory reduced `surplus labour` by shortening shifts and improving automation.

explanationZhTw: `set fire to` 是片語動詞，意思是「點火燒掉、縱火焚燒」。
generatedExample: Someone `set fire to` the old shed last night.

explanationZhTw: `hierarchical society` 指「階層分明的社會」，重點是不同階級之間有明顯上下關係。
generatedExample: The novel imagines a `hierarchical society` where status determines every opportunity.

explanationZhTw: `credulous` 指「輕信的、容易相信別人的」，這裡帶有被洗腦、缺乏懷疑能力的意思。
generatedExample: A credulous audience may accept false claims without checking the facts.

explanationZhTw: `prevailing moods` 指「主導性的情緒／占上風的心情」，這裡強調某人最常出現、最支配行為的情緒狀態。
generatedExample: Her prevailing mood was anxiety after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `mentality` 不是單純「心態」，而是指一整套符合戰爭狀態的思維方式和價值反應。
generatedExample: The team adopted the mentality appropriate to a state of war when deadlines became extreme.

explanationZhTw: `spurious` 在這裡不是單純「假的」，而是「看似真實但其實不正當、虛假的」。
generatedExample: The report was based on spurious data and had to be withdrawn.

explanationZhTw: `neutralized` 在這裡不是「中和化學物質」的字面義，而是指「把某種知識或感受的影響消除掉」。
generatedExample: The criticism was neutralized by a quick and honest explanation.

explanationZhTw: `DOUBLETHINK` 是本書中的專有政治術語，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的想法，卻不覺得衝突。
generatedExample: The regime relied on DOUBLETHINK to make citizens accept contradictions.

explanationZhTw: `wavers` 這裡是「動搖、猶豫」的意思，表示信念完全沒有鬆動。
generatedExample: She never wavers when making difficult decisions.

explanationZhTw: 「信條；不容懷疑的核心信念」；這裡表示他們把這件事當成必信的政治教義。
generatedExample: For her, helping others is an article of faith, not just a hobby.

explanationZhTw: 「逐步累積、壯大」；這裡指透過一點一點累積領土來增強力量。
generatedExample: They kept building up their savings until they could buy a house.

explanationZhTw: 「壓倒性的力量優勢」；`preponderance` 在這裡指明顯超過對手的份量或優勢。
generatedExample: The new alliance created a preponderance of power in the region.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「出口」，而是「發揮的管道、抒發的空間」。
generatedExample: Painting gave her an outlet for stress after work.

explanationZhTw: 「幾乎不復存在」；表示某種事物已接近消失。
generatedExample: That old tradition has almost ceased to exist in the city.

explanationZhTw: 「經驗式思考方法」；強調以觀察、實驗和證據為基礎。
generatedExample: An empirical method of thought helps researchers test ideas against real data.

explanationZhTw: 「人類自由的削減」；`diminution` 是較正式的「減少、縮減」。
generatedExample: The policy caused a diminution of human liberty in several ways.

explanationZhTw: 「停滯不前」；表示沒有進步。
generatedExample: Without investment, the project will just stand still.

explanationZhTw: 「倒退、退步」；表示情況比以前更差。
generatedExample: If we cut training, the team will start going backwards.

explanationZhTw: 「徹底地、永久地、一勞永逸地」；表示要完全消除某件事。
generatedExample: We need to settle this once and for all.

explanationZhTw: 「就……而言；在……範圍內」；是較正式的限制條件說法。
generatedExample: In so far as we can tell, the plan is still on track.

explanationZhTw: 「審訊者；拷問者」；在這裡暗示科學家像審問犯人一樣分析人。
generatedExample: The officer sounded more like an inquisitor than a friendly interviewer.

explanationZhTw: 「極度細緻；一絲不苟」；指非常精密地觀察細節。
generatedExample: She examined the contract with minuteness before signing it.

explanationZhTw: 「不知疲倦地」；常用來形容持續努力、毫不懈怠。
generatedExample: They worked indefatigably to finish the rescue operation.

explanationZhTw: 「後勤；物流安排」；這裡指戰爭所需的人員、物資與運送規劃。
generatedExample: The logistics of moving that many people are complicated.

explanationZhTw: 「設計；構想」；通常指為達成目的而想出一個方案或裝置。
generatedExample: Engineers devised a safer way to store the fuel.

explanationZhTw: 「無法穿透的；堅不可破的」；這裡形容裝甲非常堅硬難破。
generatedExample: The forest was so dense that it looked impenetrable.

explanationZhTw: 「使免疫於；使不受影響」；這裡是生物學語境，指對抗體有免疫性。
generatedExample: Scientists are studying bacteria immunized against common antibiotics.

explanationZhTw: 「鑽行前進；硬生生穿過」；這裡形容載具像鑽頭一樣在地下前進。
generatedExample: The drill bore its way through the rock.

explanationZhTw: 表示「幾乎接近實現」；這裡強調這些計畫連成功的邊都碰不到。
generatedExample: Her proposal sounded ambitious, but it never came anywhere near realization.

explanationZhTw: 指「明顯的領先優勢」或「大幅度的領先」。
generatedExample: Our team had a significant lead after the first half.

explanationZhTw: 表示「照它一貫的習慣」；這裡暗示政黨總是把功勞攬到自己身上。
generatedExample: According to its habit, the company blamed the delay on weather.

explanationZhTw: 指「大規模地」或「廣泛地」。
generatedExample: The medicine was distributed on a large scale after the outbreak.

explanationZhTw: 指「統治階層」或「掌權者」。
generatedExample: The ruling groups ignored the warning signs for years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「儲備起來、囤積起來」。
generatedExample: They store food up against winter.

explanationZhTw: 指「關鍵時機」或「決定成敗的機會」。
generatedExample: We are waiting for a decisive opportunity to act.

explanationZhTw: 表示「遲早」；強調某事終將發生。
generatedExample: Sooner or later, you will need to make a decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stationary 不是「車站的」，而是「幾乎沒有變化、停滯不前」。
generatedExample: Technology in that field remained almost stationary for decades.

explanationZhTw: 指「被取代、代替」。
generatedExample: Old paper maps have been superseded by navigation apps.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「讓位給、被取代成」。
generatedExample: Tradition has given way to convenience in many places.

explanationZhTw: 表示「儘管、雖然」。
generatedExample: In spite of the rain, the match continued.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `manoeuvre` 不是單純的「動作」，而是指有策略性的軍事行動或運作手段。
generatedExample: The army planned a clever manoeuvre to avoid heavy losses.

explanationZhTw: `undertaken` 在這裡表示「被執行、被展開」，不是「承擔責任」的字面感，而是指一個大型行動開始進行。
generatedExample: The project was undertaken by a small team of engineers.

explanationZhTw: `treachery` 指「背叛、出賣」，帶有強烈的負面語氣，常用於政治或戰爭語境。
generatedExample: The spy was punished for treachery against his country.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「讓懷疑慢慢消失、讓人放鬆警戒」，不是字面上的「把懷疑哄睡」。
generatedExample: They kept making friendly promises to lull public suspicion to sleep.

explanationZhTw: `simultaneously` 表示「同時地」，在正式書寫和說明文中很常見。
generatedExample: All the alarms went off simultaneously.

explanationZhTw: `retaliation` 指「報復、反擊」，多用於衝突、戰爭或嚴重爭端。
generatedExample: The government warned that any attack would lead to retaliation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `daydream` 不是「作白日夢」的中性意思，而是指「不切實際、根本不可能實現的想法」。
generatedExample: His plan to finish everything in one day was just a daydream.

explanationZhTw: `arbitrary` 在這裡是「任意的、沒有明確規則或理由的」意思。
generatedExample: The border was arbitrary and had little to do with the landscape.

explanationZhTw: `violate` 這裡指「違反、破壞」某個原則或規範。
generatedExample: This decision would violate the terms of the agreement.

explanationZhTw: `exterminate` 指「徹底消滅、殲滅」，語氣非常強烈，常用於暴力或極端情境。
generatedExample: The invading force tried to exterminate the local population.

explanationZhTw: `assimilate` 在這裡是「同化、使融入另一個社會或文化」，不是單純的「理解」。
generatedExample: New arrivals were expected to assimilate quickly into the local culture.

explanationZhTw: `sets eyes on` 是慣用語，意思是「看見、見到」，常帶有第一次見到或罕見看到的語感。
generatedExample: I have never set eyes on that man before.

explanationZhTw: `forbidden` 表示「被禁止的」，這裡是被權力系統限制接觸外語知識。
generatedExample: Students are forbidden to use their phones during the exam.

explanationZhTw: `evaporate` 原意是「蒸發」，這裡比喻成情緒或態度「消失、不見了」。
generatedExample: Once the truth came out, their confidence evaporated.

explanationZhTw: `change hands` 是慣用語，意思是「易手、轉到另一方控制之下」，常用於領土、財產或權力。
generatedExample: The old house changed hands several times before being renovated.

explanationZhTw: `acted upon` 在這裡是「被採納並付諸實行」的意思。整句是在說：這個事實雖然沒明講，但大家默默接受並照做。
generatedExample: The safety advice was acted upon immediately.

explanationZhTw: `prevailing` 在此不是「獲勝的」，而是「盛行的、占主導地位的」。`prevailing philosophy` 指當前主流的思想體系。
generatedExample: The prevailing philosophy in the company is to move fast and test often.

explanationZhTw: `tenets` 指「信條、核心原則、教義」。這裡是指另外兩種思想體系的基本主張。
generatedExample: One of the tenets of the course is to learn by doing.

explanationZhTw: `execrate` 是「痛恨、唾棄、詛咒」的正式說法。這裡表示被教育成要把對方思想當成極可憎的東西。
generatedExample: Some critics execrate the new policy as pointless and cruel.

explanationZhTw: `barbarous outrages` 是強烈貶抑的說法，意思接近「野蠻、殘暴又嚴重冒犯道德與常識的行為」。
generatedExample: They described the attack as a barbarous outrage on civilians.

explanationZhTw: `barely distinguishable` 表示「幾乎分不出來」。這裡強調三種哲學其實非常相似。
generatedExample: The two samples were barely distinguishable under normal light.

explanationZhTw: `pyramidal structure` 指「金字塔式結構」，也就是底層人數多、越往上越少的階層體制。
generatedExample: The startup had a pyramidal structure with a very small executive team.

explanationZhTw: `prop one another up` 是片語動詞，意思是「互相支撐、互相維持」。這裡指三個超級國家彼此敵對，反而讓對方繼續存在。
generatedExample: The two failing companies propped one another up for years.

explanationZhTw: `aware and unaware` 形成刻意的矛盾效果，表示他們一方面知道真相，一方面又選擇忽視真相。
generatedExample: He was aware and unaware of the risk at the same time.

explanationZhTw: `dedicated to` 在這裡是「全心投入、以...為使命」的意思，不只是一般的「奉獻」。
generatedExample: She is dedicated to rebuilding the neighborhood library.

explanationZhTw: `everlastingly` 是較文學化的說法，意思是「永遠地、無止境地」。
generatedExample: They argued everlastingly about who should lead the project.

explanationZhTw: `denial of reality` 在此是指「否認現實、扭曲現實」的心理或政治狀態。
generatedExample: The rumor spread because people preferred denial of reality to bad news.

explanationZhTw: 「came to an end」表示「結束、告終」，常用在事件、狀態或關係收尾的情況。
generatedExample: The meeting came to an end after three hours.

explanationZhTw: 「keep in touch with」在這裡不是單純聯絡，而是指「與...保持連結、保持接觸」。
generatedExample: I try to keep in touch with my old classmates.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「付不起錢」，而是「承擔不起風險／不敢冒這個代價」。
generatedExample: We could not afford to make a mistake at this stage.

explanationZhTw: 「tended to impair」表示「容易／有傾向地削弱、妨害」，語氣比直接說 damage 更正式。
generatedExample: Too little sleep tends to impair concentration.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是隱喻說法，表示在設計武器或飛機時必須完全符合事實與數學，不容含糊或自欺。
generatedExample: For this project, the numbers have to make four before we launch.

explanationZhTw: 正式用語，表示「對...有害、與...不相容、會妨礙...」。
generatedExample: Lack of sleep is inimical to good decision-making.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「coloured」不是顏色，而是「帶有立場影響、不夠中立」；整體意思是內容有偏向。
generatedExample: His description of the event was coloured and biased.

explanationZhTw: 固定說法，表示「就統治階級而言／從統治階級的角度看」。
generatedExample: So far as I was concerned, the plan was too risky.

explanationZhTw: 「palpable」在這裡不是指「可觸摸的」，而是「明顯到幾乎摸得到」的意思；整體指非常清楚、無法否認的事實。
generatedExample: The evidence was palpable facts, even though he tried to ignore it.

explanationZhTw: 「carry out」表示「執行、進行」；這裡是指研究仍然被實際做下去。
generatedExample: The survey was carried out by a small team last week.

explanationZhTw: 「in effect」表示「實際上、就結果而言」，不一定完全等於字面上的「效果」。
generatedExample: In effect, the new rule gives everyone one extra day to finish.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「思想被扭曲、變形的狀態」，不是一般日常用法中的「歪曲」那麼簡單，而是帶有政治控制下的異常思維。
generatedExample: The novel shows how fear can lead to a perversion of thought.

explanationZhTw: 「cut off from」是片語動詞，表示「與……隔絕、切斷聯繫」。
generatedExample: After the storm, the village was cut off from the city for two days.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「恆星間的太空」；在這裡用來比喻完全失去外界參照、方向感全無的處境。
generatedExample: Without guidance, he felt like he was drifting in interstellar space.

explanationZhTw: 「be obliged to」表示「不得不、被迫」；這裡強調統治者也有某些不得不做的事。
generatedExample: We were obliged to leave early because the train was canceled.

explanationZhTw: 「be obliged to」表示「不得不、被迫」；這裡強調統治者也有某些不得不做的事。
generatedExample: We were obliged to leave early because the train was canceled.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「扭曲現實物體」，而是指「竄改事實、操控現實敘事」的意思。
generatedExample: The propaganda tries to twist reality into something harmless.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `imposture` 不是「姿態」，而是指「假象、騙局、偽裝成真的東西」。
generatedExample: The report was an imposture, hiding the real losses.

explanationZhTw: `eats up` 是片語動詞，意思是「大量消耗、吞掉」；這裡不是字面上的吃。
generatedExample: The new software eats up a lot of memory.

explanationZhTw: `surplus` 指「剩餘、過剩的部分」，常用在資源、商品或金錢上。
generatedExample: The factory sold its surplus equipment.

explanationZhTw: `consumable goods` 指「可消耗的商品」或「會被用掉的物資」。
generatedExample: The warehouse stores consumable goods for the winter.

explanationZhTw: `hierarchical` 表示「有階層的、分上下等級的」；`hierarchical society` 就是「階層分明的社會」。
generatedExample: A hierarchical society often gives more power to those at the top.

explanationZhTw: `internal affair` 在這裡是「內部事務、內部問題」，不是一般的日常瑣事。
generatedExample: The dispute became an internal affair of the company.

explanationZhTw: `ruling groups` 指「統治階層、掌權集團」，也就是掌控政治權力的人。
generatedExample: The ruling groups decided to change the law.

explanationZhTw: `plundered` 意思是「搶掠、洗劫」，通常帶有戰爭或暴力情境。
generatedExample: The invaders plundered the village.

explanationZhTw: `vanquished` 指「被擊敗的、戰敗的」，是較正式或文學性的說法。
generatedExample: The vanquished army withdrew at dawn.

explanationZhTw: `waged` 在 `war is waged` 裡表示「進行、發動（戰爭）」；這是固定搭配，不是單純的「支付工資」之類意思。
generatedExample: A long campaign was waged against corruption.

explanationZhTw: `subjects` 在政治語境裡是「臣民、被統治者」，不是一般的「主題」。
generatedExample: The king taxed his subjects heavily.

explanationZhTw: `intact` 意思是「完整無損的、未被破壞的」。
generatedExample: The ancient wall remained intact after the earthquake.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `continuous war` 是「持續不斷的戰爭」這種抽象概念，重點在於戰爭變成永久狀態。
generatedExample: The region lived under continuous war for decades.

explanationZhTw: `exerted` 在這裡是「施加、產生（壓力、影響）」；常見於 `exert pressure/influence`。
generatedExample: The deadline exerted pressure on the team.

explanationZhTw: `perpetual peace` 指「永久和平、永無止境的和平」，屬於較抽象的政治表達。
generatedExample: They hoped for perpetual peace after the treaty.

explanationZhTw: `inviolate` 意思是「未受侵犯的、完好無損的」，很正式。
generatedExample: The borders were kept inviolate by the agreement.

explanationZhTw: `self-contained` 這裡不是「自給自足」的日常用法而已，而是指「完全封閉、獨立運作的整體」。
generatedExample: The island became a self-contained universe for the research team.

explanationZhTw: `sobering influence` 指「讓人清醒、冷靜下來的作用」；這裡強調外在危險會迫使人保持現實感。
generatedExample: Failure can have a sobering influence on overconfident people.

explanationZhTw: `inner meaning` 是「內在含義、深層意義」，和表面字義不同。
generatedExample: I finally understood the inner meaning of the poem.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的專有詞，指兼具電視與監視功能的裝置；在這裡是反烏托邦世界的特殊用法。
generatedExample: There was a telescreen in every room, so privacy was impossible.

explanationZhTw: `wear off` 表示「逐漸消失、減弱」；這裡是指那種愉悅感還沒有退去。
generatedExample: The excitement of the trip had not worn off by the next morning.

explanationZhTw: `reassure` 在這裡是「使放心、減輕不安」；語氣比一般的「安慰」更偏向消除疑慮。
generatedExample: Her calm answer reassured him that everything was under control.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用表達，意思是把混亂的想法整理清楚、理出頭緒。
generatedExample: I need a quiet place to set my thoughts in order before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `perceived` 不是單純「看見」，而是較正式的「察覺到、理解到」。
generatedExample: She perceived that the plan would fail unless they changed it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是中性的「放下」，而是帶有隨手、粗重地丟下的意思。
generatedExample: He dumped his backpack by the door and rushed inside.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，表示非常迅速、熱烈地投入對方懷抱。
generatedExample: When she saw him at the station, she flung herself into his arms.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `got` 不是「得到」的完成式重點，而是口語中的 `have`，意思是「我有／我拿到了」。在這句話裡，他是在確認自己手上有那本書。
generatedExample: I've got the tickets in my bag.

explanationZhTw: `disentangled themselves` 指「把自己從糾纏、擁擠或交纏的狀態中分開、脫離出來」。這裡是很具體的動作描寫，不是日常最直覺的用法。
generatedExample: After the concert, the crowd slowly disentangled themselves from the exit.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `you've got it` 是口語用法，意思是「你明白了／你拿到了」。在這句裡比較像是「你弄懂了嗎？」
generatedExample: If you've got it, we can move on to the next step.

explanationZhTw: `interest` 這裡不是「利息」或一般的「興趣」，而是指「關注度、熱情」。整個片語表示她說話時沒有太多在意或熱忱。
generatedExample: He listened without much interest and kept checking his phone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `pull up` 是片語動詞，意思是「把某物往上拉來蓋好」，指把被子拉上來保暖，不是單純「往上拉」的動作。
generatedExample: She pulled up the blanket when the wind got colder.

explanationZhTw: `counterpane` 是較文學或舊式的說法，意思是「床罩、被面」；現代英文更常用 `blanket` 或 `bedspread`。
generatedExample: She smoothed the counterpane before turning off the light.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fixture` 是比喻用法，指「幾乎成了固定班底、常駐的人」，不是字面上的「裝置、固定物」。
generatedExample: After a few months, he became a fixture at the local bookstore.

explanationZhTw: `lusty` 在這裡是「響亮而有力的、充滿活力的」意思，形容歌聲很有勁，不是字面上只指肉體的慾望。
generatedExample: The crowd joined in a lusty singing of the anthem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指《1984》中的秘密組織「兄弟會」，不是一般的「兄弟情誼」。
generatedExample: She claimed that the Brotherhood was still active in the city.

explanationZhTw: 表示「必須、不得不」，是常見的義務表達。
generatedExample: You have to finish the form before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 「aloud」表示「出聲地、唸出來」；在這裡是要把文字直接念出聲音，而不是默讀。
generatedExample: Please read the paragraph aloud so everyone can hear it.

explanationZhTw: 「as you go」在這裡是「一邊進行一邊做、同時進行」的意思，不是字面上的「你走路時」。
generatedExample: I like to take notes as I go, so I do not forget anything.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `meaning eighteen` 是在解釋前面的時間表示法，指的是 18:00，也就是傍晚六點。這屬於語境中的特殊用法，不是字面上的「意思是十八」那麼簡單。
generatedExample: The sign said 08:00, meaning eight in the morning.

explanationZhTw: `ahead of them` 在這裡表示「他們前面還有」或「他們還有」幾個小時可用，意思是時間上還有餘裕。
generatedExample: We still had two hours ahead of us before the train left.

explanationZhTw: `propped ... against ...` 表示把某物支撐、靠放在另一個物體上，讓它保持穩定。這裡是把書靠在膝蓋上。
generatedExample: She propped her phone against a cup and started the video call.

explanationZhTw: 這是口號式表達，表面上是「無知就是力量」，但在《1984》裡是極權政權的宣傳語，帶有反諷意味；它不是在陳述一般事實，而是在操控人的思考。
generatedExample: The slogan looked simple, but in context it was a tool to control how people thought.

explanationZhTw: 「Neolithic Age」是「新石器時代」，屬於歷史時期名稱。這裡用來表示人類社會自史前時代以來就存在分層結構。
generatedExample: Archaeologists found tools from the Neolithic Age near the river.

explanationZhTw: 「subdivided」意思是「再細分、再分類」。這裡指三種人又被各種方式進一步劃分。
generatedExample: The class was subdivided into four smaller groups.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「borne」是動詞 bear 的過去分詞，意思不是「承載」實物，而是「帶有、擁有」許多不同名稱。
generatedExample: The town has borne several names over the centuries.

explanationZhTw: 「upheavals」指「劇變、動盪」。這裡強調即使經歷巨大社會或政治變動，基本結構仍然不變。
generatedExample: The country went through political upheavals last year.

explanationZhTw: 「irrevocable」表示「不可撤銷的、無法挽回的」。這裡形容變化看起來已經定局，不能再回頭。
generatedExample: She made an irrevocable decision to move abroad.

explanationZhTw: 「reasserted itself」意思是「再次顯現、重新恢復原狀」。這裡指同樣的社會模式又回來了。
generatedExample: After a brief pause, the old problem reasserted itself.

explanationZhTw: 「equilibrium」是「平衡、均衡」；在這裡是比喻性的說法，指系統會回到穩定狀態。
generatedExample: The market is slowly returning to equilibrium.

explanationZhTw: 「awake」在這裡是形容詞，表示「清醒的、沒睡著的」。這是常見的基礎詞，但在對話中用來直接確認對方是否還醒著。
generatedExample: Are you awake, or did you fall asleep?

explanationZhTw: 「Go on」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「繼續說、接著講下去」。
generatedExample: Please go on; I want to hear the rest of your idea.

explanationZhTw: 「marvellous」表示「非常棒、極好」，屬於較正式、帶一點英式語感的用字。
generatedExample: The view from the hill was marvellous at sunset.

explanationZhTw: 「irreconcilable」指彼此無法調和、無法並存。在這裡形容三個群體的目標根本衝突。
generatedExample: The two proposals are irreconcilable.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「維持原位、維持現狀」，不是單純的「留下來」而已。
generatedExample: In this role, I will remain where I am.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的換位置，而是指取代高層、成為新的掌權者。
generatedExample: The new team wants to change places with the current leaders.

explanationZhTw: 「abiding」在這裡是「長期存在的、固定不變的」。整個片語表示某種持續不變的特徵。
generatedExample: It is an abiding rule in our house to remove your shoes.

explanationZhTw: 「drudgery」指單調、繁重又乏味的苦差事。這裡強調他們被日常勞役壓得沒有餘力思考別的事。
generatedExample: The job was full of drudgery and little reward.

explanationZhTw: 「intermittently」是斷斷續續地；這裡表示只能間歇性地意識到外界。
generatedExample: She was only intermittently conscious during the night shift.

explanationZhTw: 「abolish」是廢除；「distinctions」在這裡指階級、差別或區分。整句是在說要消除一切差別。
generatedExample: They want to abolish all distinctions between departments.

explanationZhTw: 表示「整個歷史長河中、從古至今」。這裡用來概括反覆出現的權力鬥爭。
generatedExample: Throughout history, people have migrated for safety.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「穩穩掌權、地位牢固」的意思。
generatedExample: After the election, she felt securely in power.

explanationZhTw: 「overthrow」指推翻政權或權威；這裡是被中間階層推翻。
generatedExample: The old government was overthrown in a week.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「拉攏、爭取某人支持」的意思，不是字面上的參軍。
generatedExample: She tried to enlist her friends on her side.

explanationZhTw: 「objective」是目標、目的。這裡指他們達成奪權目的。
generatedExample: Our main objective is to finish on time.

explanationZhTw: 「thrust back」在這裡表示強行推回、打回原位。
generatedExample: The manager thrust the proposal back at us.

explanationZhTw: 「servitude」指奴役、隸屬狀態。這裡表示低層又被壓回受制的位置。
generatedExample: The workers lived in servitude for years.

explanationZhTw: 「split off」是分裂出去、脫離出來。這裡指新的中間階層從原有群體中形成。
generatedExample: A small team splits off from the main project.

explanationZhTw: 表示「暫時成功的」，強調連短暫的成功都沒有。
generatedExample: The fix was only temporarily successful.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「material」不是「材料的」，而是「物質層面、實際層面」的意思。
generatedExample: The new policy brought only material benefits.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指風俗、舉止、社會習氣變得比較溫和或文明。
generatedExample: People noticed a softening of manners in the city.

explanationZhTw: 這是常見片語，表示「從某人的角度來看」。
generatedExample: From my point of view, the plan is too risky.

explanationZhTw: 表示「很明顯、容易看出來」；這裡說這種模式已經讓許多觀察者看得出來。
generatedExample: It was obvious to everyone that the meeting would run late.

explanationZhTw: 指「循環式的過程」；這裡把歷史描述成會不斷重複循環，而不是直線前進。
generatedExample: She thinks the economy follows a cyclical process.

explanationZhTw: 指「階層式的」；社會被分成高低階層，權力分布不平等。
generatedExample: The company has a hierarchical structure.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，字面是「在旗幟之下」，這裡指「以……為名、打著……的旗號」。
generatedExample: They campaigned under the banner of reform.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「源自於、由……發展而來」。
generatedExample: The app grew out of a small side project.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「口頭上支持但沒有真正落實」。
generatedExample: He pays lip-service to teamwork but never listens.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「逮捕」，而是「阻止、抑制」；arrest progress 就是阻止進步。
generatedExample: Cold weather can arrest plant growth.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡指「被趕下台／被清除出去」；不是一般的「結果是」。
generatedExample: The manager was turned out after the scandal.

explanationZhTw: 「累積」；這裡指歷史知識逐漸堆積，讓新的政治觀念有了基礎。
generatedExample: The accumulation of small errors eventually caused the system to fail.

explanationZhTw: 「可理解的；可被理解」；在這裡是說歷史循環已經能被看懂、解釋。
generatedExample: Her explanation made the whole situation intelligible.

explanationZhTw: 「可理解的；可被理解」；在這裡是說歷史循環已經能被看懂、解釋。
generatedExample: Her explanation made the whole situation intelligible.

explanationZhTw: 「可改變的」；表示既然看得懂，就可能進一步改變。
generatedExample: The plan is still alterable if we act quickly.

explanationZhTw: 「在技術上已經做得到」；不是指道德上或政治上已經實現，而是客觀條件已具備。
generatedExample: A fully electric ferry is technically possible with current batteries.

explanationZhTw: 「專門化」；指工作或職能被分工成不同專業。
generatedExample: The roles in the team became highly specialized.

explanationZhTw: 「即將、快要」；表示某事已經到了發生前一刻。
generatedExample: She was on the point of answering when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: 「值得努力追求」；這是較書面、老派的說法。
generatedExample: A stable peace is something to be striven after.

explanationZhTw: 「避免；防止」；表示要把某種危險擋開。
generatedExample: Quick action averted a serious accident.

explanationZhTw: 「人間天堂」；指理想中的完美社會或烏托邦。
generatedExample: Many reformers have dreamed of an earthly paradise on earth.

explanationZhTw: 「粗重勞役」；這裡強調辛苦、原始、非人性的體力勞動。
generatedExample: They were forced into brute labour in the mines.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「鬧鬼」，而是「長期縈繞在腦中；揮之不去」。
generatedExample: The memory of that mistake haunted him for years.

explanationZhTw: 「威權的；獨裁傾向的」；指強調服從權威、限制自由的政治立場。
generatedExample: The new government became increasingly authoritarian.

explanationZhTw: 「失去信譽；被否定」；表示原本受重視的觀念被人不再相信。
generatedExample: The theory was discredited after the experiment failed.

explanationZhTw: 「可實現的」；指原本只是理想的事，現在已經有可能做到。
generatedExample: Their long-term goal is now realizable.

explanationZhTw: 「導回；最終回到」；這裡表示不管叫什麼名字，最後都指向同一結果。
generatedExample: The discussion kept leading back to the same issue.

explanationZhTw: 「嚴密管制；軍事化式的管束」；強調制度變得刻板、服從性高。
generatedExample: The school rejected excessive regimentation.

explanationZhTw: 「開始出現；漸漸形成」；常用來描述某種情況、氛圍或變化逐步發生。
generatedExample: By evening, fatigue had set in.

explanationZhTw: 「驅逐出境；大規模遞解」；通常指把人強制送離某地或國家。
generatedExample: The deportation of refugees would have serious humanitarian consequences.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「開明的；思想進步的」，不是字面上的「發光的」。
generatedExample: He saw himself as an enlightened leader who welcomed debate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `emerged` 是「浮現、成形、出現」的意思，表示這些政治理論是在長期混亂之後才逐漸發展成熟，不是單純「走出來」。
generatedExample: After months of debate, a clear plan finally emerged.

explanationZhTw: `foreshadowed` 表示「預示、提前顯示可能發生的事」，這裡是在說後來的政治理論其實早已被前面的制度型態預先顯現出來。
generatedExample: The warning signs foreshadowed a bigger problem later.

explanationZhTw: `totalitarian` 是「極權的」，指政府或制度對社會、思想與行為有全面控制。
generatedExample: They lived under a totalitarian regime that controlled the media.

explanationZhTw: `aristocracy` 原本是「貴族階級」，這裡是比喻性的用法，指新形成、掌握權力的一群上層統治者。
generatedExample: A new political aristocracy began to dominate the country.

explanationZhTw: `avaricious` 意思是「貪婪的、貪財的」，比一般的 `greedy` 更正式，也更偏書面語。
generatedExample: The king was portrayed as avaricious and cruel.

explanationZhTw: `crushing opposition` 不是字面上「壓碎反對」，而是指「徹底打壓反對力量」，屬於語境中的強烈表達。
generatedExample: The government was accused of crushing opposition during the election.

explanationZhTw: `leave loose ends` 是慣用語，表示「留下未解決的細節、後患或收尾不完整的地方」。
generatedExample: Before we launch, we need to tie up the loose ends.

explanationZhTw: `under constant surveillance` 指「持續受到監視」，這裡是政治語境中的重要表達。
generatedExample: The suspect remained under constant surveillance.

explanationZhTw: `manipulate public opinion` 指「操縱輿論」，不是一般的「影響」，而是有意地引導大眾想法。
generatedExample: The campaign was designed to manipulate public opinion.

explanationZhTw: `uniformity of opinion` 指「意見完全一致」；在這裡強調的是思想被強制變得一致，而不是自然達成共識。
generatedExample: The school valued discussion, not uniformity of opinion.

explanationZhTw: forerunners 指「先行者、前例、前幾代的同類事物」。這裡是指前面那些統治階層。
generatedExample: The new policy was very different from its forerunners.

explanationZhTw: safeguard 在這裡是「鞏固、保住」地位，不只是一般的「保護」。
generatedExample: The company invested in new technology to safeguard its position in the market.

explanationZhTw: oligarchy 指「寡頭政治」，也就是由少數人掌權的政治體制。
generatedExample: The country was criticized for becoming an oligarchy.

explanationZhTw: collectivism 指「集體主義」，強調群體利益高於個人利益。
generatedExample: In that community, collectivism was valued more than individual success.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是政治語境中的反諷用法；表面上是「廢除私有財產」，實際上是把財產集中到更少數的人手中。
generatedExample: The regime announced the abolition of private property, but ownership quickly shifted to a small elite.

explanationZhTw: step into 這裡是「順勢進入、接手」；整個片語指「取得這個支配性地位」。
generatedExample: After the merger, she stepped into a commanding position in the company.

explanationZhTw: expropriate 指「剝奪財產、沒收」，常見於政治或法律語境。這裡是指把資產從資本家手中拿走。
generatedExample: The state expropriated the land for public use.

explanationZhTw: phraseology 指「措辭、用語風格」；這裡表示沿用了早期社會主義的說法。
generatedExample: The report inherited its phraseology from older official documents.

explanationZhTw: foreseen 在這裡不只是「預見」，而是強調這個結果其實早就被設計好、預先安排。
generatedExample: The consequences were foreseen and intended beforehand by the planners.

explanationZhTw: 指「階層化的社會」，也就是社會被明顯分成上下層級的結構。
generatedExample: The novel explores how a hierarchical society controls who gets power.

explanationZhTw: 指「失去政權／倒台」，不是字面上「從權力上掉下來」，而是政治上被迫下台。
generatedExample: After the scandal, the minister quickly fell from power.

explanationZhTw: 指「形成、誕生、出現」，常用來說某個制度、團體或概念開始存在。
generatedExample: A new movement came into being after the election.

explanationZhTw: 指「自信」，這裡特別強調統治階層對自己能否繼續治理失去信心。
generatedExample: She spoke with enough self-confidence to answer every question.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「單獨起作用／各自獨立發生」，不是一般機械或商業上的 operate。
generatedExample: The problems rarely operate singly; they usually appear together.

explanationZhTw: 指「通常、一般而言」，是用來表示大多數情況如此，但不是絕對。
generatedExample: As a rule, we meet on Mondays.

explanationZhTw: 指「防範、預防」，常見搭配，意思是採取措施避免壞事發生。
generatedExample: Wearing sunscreen helps guard against skin damage.

explanationZhTw: 指「決定性因素」，也就是最後真正左右結果的關鍵原因。
generatedExample: Cost was the determining factor in our decision.

explanationZhTw: 指「心理態度、心態」，這裡表示統治階層對局勢的想法與心態。
generatedExample: A positive mental attitude can help people handle stress.

explanationZhTw: 表示「事實上、實際上」，用來強調和表面印象不同的真實情況。
generatedExample: In reality, the problem was much smaller than we first thought.

explanationZhTw: 表示「其實、事實上」，用來修正或強化前面的說法。
generatedExample: The task looks simple, but in fact it takes a lot of skill.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不可征服的、無法被擊敗的」；這裡指幾乎不可能被推翻。
generatedExample: The army considered the mountain fortress unconquerable.

explanationZhTw: 指人口結構或人口數量的變化。
generatedExample: Demographic changes are affecting housing demand in the city.

explanationZhTw: 表示「自發地、自願地」，不是被迫或被安排的。
generatedExample: He apologized of his own accord before anyone asked him to.

explanationZhTw: 指用來比較的基準；這裡是說人們沒有比較對象，就難以察覺差距。
generatedExample: Without standards of comparison, it is hard to judge whether the service is good.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「脫臼」，而是指社會、經濟或秩序上的嚴重失調與混亂。
generatedExample: The storm caused major dislocations in transportation and trade.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「清楚表達出來、變得能被說出口」；不是指「口齒清晰」的字面意思。
generatedExample: People may feel frustrated but still struggle to become articulate about it.

explanationZhTw: 表示「潛伏的、潛在的」；問題一直存在，只是尚未明顯浮現。
generatedExample: There was a latent conflict between the two departments.

explanationZhTw: 表示「使情緒高漲、使人緊張或興奮起來」；這裡是把士氣拉高。
generatedExample: The coach keyed up the team before the final match.

explanationZhTw: 指大眾的士氣、信心和精神狀態。
generatedExample: The announcement was meant to improve public morale after the setback.

explanationZhTw: 表示「從……的角度來看」。
generatedExample: From the point of view of the customer, the delay was unacceptable.

explanationZhTw: 表示「就業不足」或「工作未充分發揮能力」，通常指有工作但工時或職責不足。
generatedExample: Many skilled workers are under-employed in this market.

explanationZhTw: 指「渴望權力的、野心很大的」，帶貶義。
generatedExample: The novel follows a power-hungry minister who will do anything to stay in control.

explanationZhTw: 表示「也就是說、換句話說」，用來重新表述前一句。
generatedExample: We need a new strategy, that is to say, one that actually changes behavior.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「塑造、形塑」思想或態度，不是做成實體形狀。
generatedExample: The school is moulding students' habits from an early age.

explanationZhTw: 指意識、認知，也可指對某事的覺察與思維方式。
generatedExample: Public consciousness about food waste has increased.

explanationZhTw: 指「大眾、群眾」，是把很多普通人當成一個整體來說。
generatedExample: The campaign was designed to appeal to the masses.

explanationZhTw: 「infer」是「推斷、推論」；根據已知背景去得出結論，語氣比單純的「know」更進一步。
generatedExample: From the evidence, we can infer that the meeting was canceled.

explanationZhTw: 「apex」是「頂點、最高點」；這裡用金字塔比喻權力結構，表示最上層。
generatedExample: The CEO sits at the apex of the company hierarchy.

explanationZhTw: 「infallible」是「絕對不會錯的、無誤的」；常用來形容人或權威被神化後的形象。
generatedExample: The old speaker was treated as infallible by his followers.

explanationZhTw: 「all-powerful」是「全能的、擁有至高權力的」；這裡強調 Big Brother 被塑造成無所不能。
generatedExample: In the story, the king is portrayed as all-powerful.

explanationZhTw: 「issue directly from」在這裡是「直接源自、來自」；不是一般名詞「issue」，而是表示起源或產生。
generatedExample: The team’s confidence was said to issue directly from their coach’s support.

explanationZhTw: 「hoardings」在英式英文裡指「招牌廣告板、圍板廣告」；這裡是政治宣傳圖像出現的地方。
generatedExample: Posters were pasted all over the hoardings near the station.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是小說中的詞，指會播放影像、也可能監視人的螢幕裝置；屬於作品內的特殊用語。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the novel never stopped watching the citizens.

explanationZhTw: 「reasonably sure」是「相當確定」；表示有足夠把握，但不是百分之百。
generatedExample: I’m reasonably sure the train leaves at 8:10.

explanationZhTw: 「considerable uncertainty」是「相當大的不確定性」；這裡強調連他的出生時間都說不清楚。
generatedExample: There is considerable uncertainty about the final cost of the project.

explanationZhTw: 「guise」是「外表、偽裝、表象」；這裡指政黨用 Big Brother 這個形象對外呈現自己。
generatedExample: He entered the party in the guise of a delivery driver.

explanationZhTw: 「exhibit itself」這裡不是字面上的「展覽自己」，而是「向外呈現、表現出來」的意思。
generatedExample: The company wants to exhibit itself as a responsible employer.

explanationZhTw: 「focusing point」是「聚焦點、匯集中心」；這裡指大家的情感都集中到他身上。
generatedExample: The statue became a focusing point for the crowd’s anger.

explanationZhTw: 「reverence」是「敬畏、崇敬」；比一般的 respect 更強，帶有近乎宗教式的尊崇。
generatedExample: The elders were treated with reverence by the villagers.

explanationZhTw: 「the Inner Party」是小說中的專有名詞，指政權核心菁英層；屬於作品內制度術語。
generatedExample: The Inner Party controlled the state from behind closed doors.

explanationZhTw: 「the Outer Party」也是小說中的專有名詞，指較外圍的黨員階層。
generatedExample: Members of the Outer Party had fewer privileges and more surveillance.

explanationZhTw: 「dumb masses」在這裡不是單純「沉默的人群」，而是帶貶抑的「愚鈍大眾」。
generatedExample: The speech dismissed the public as a dumb mass that could be manipulated.

explanationZhTw: 「habitually refer to」是「習慣稱作、慣常稱為」；表示這是一種固定叫法。
generatedExample: We habitually refer to this area as the old town.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」是小說中的用語，指社會底層的大眾；來自作品的特殊詞彙。
generatedExample: The proles in the novel were largely ignored by the ruling class.

explanationZhTw: 世襲的、遺傳的；這裡指身分不是靠父母自動繼承而來。
generatedExample: His talent for music is hereditary.

explanationZhTw: 名義上的領袖；只有頭銜，未必真正掌權。
generatedExample: The president is only a titular head in that system.

explanationZhTw: 通用語；不同語言族群之間用來溝通的共同語言。
generatedExample: English often serves as a lingua franca in international meetings.

explanationZhTw: 遵循、堅持；這裡指因共同信念而維繫在一起。
generatedExample: Strict adherence to the rules is required here.

explanationZhTw: 分層的；社會或組織按階層分開。
generatedExample: The company is stratified into several management levels.

explanationZhTw: 分層的；社會或組織按階層分開。
generatedExample: The company is stratified into several management levels.

explanationZhTw: 來回往返；這裡指不同群體之間的人員流動。
generatedExample: We spent the afternoon in constant to-and-fro between the two offices.

explanationZhTw: 核心、中心；這裡不是生物學上的「細胞核」，而是指不滿情緒的核心人物或中心。
generatedExample: Small groups of students became nuclei of protest on campus.

explanationZhTw: 被標記、被記錄下來；這裡帶有「被盯上／被列入名單」的意思。
generatedExample: The suspect was marked down by investigators after the interview.

explanationZhTw: 寡頭政治；少數人掌權的統治形式。
generatedExample: The country slowly moved from monarchy to oligarchy.

explanationZhTw: 使持續存在、延續；這裡是指政權要讓自己長久維持下去。
generatedExample: The policy mainly serves to perpetuating the problem.

explanationZhTw: 使持續存在、延續；這裡是指政權要讓自己長久維持下去。
generatedExample: The policy mainly serves to perpetuating the problem.

explanationZhTw: 指一種神祕、難以看穿的氣氛或形象；這裡是政黨刻意營造出的神秘感，不是一般的『魅力』。
generatedExample: The museum has a mystique that makes people curious before they even enter.

explanationZhTw: 指無產階級、勞動階層；這裡是政治語境中的『工人群眾』。
generatedExample: The novel follows the lives of proletarians in a rapidly changing city.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示『任其自然、放著不管』；這裡強調如果不干預，他們就會照原樣延續下去。
generatedExample: Left to themselves, the children would play all afternoon without any rules.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指『抓住』，而是指『理解、領會』；整句是在說他們沒有能力理解世界可以不同。
generatedExample: It took me a while to grasp the point of her argument.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是『被視為、被看作』。
generatedExample: His silence was looked on as agreement by the rest of the group.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 deviation 指『偏離、脫離標準/立場』；整體是指思想或立場上的任何偏差。
generatedExample: The manager treated any deviation of opinion from the plan as a problem.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在……監視之下」，帶有持續被盯著、沒有隱私的壓迫感。
