explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是即使只有自己一個人，也仍然站在少數意見那邊。
generatedExample: She kept defending the idea, even when she felt like a minority of one.

explanationZhTw: 「cling to」表示緊緊抓住、堅持不放；這裡是指堅持真理。
generatedExample: He clung to his plan even when everyone else doubted it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「statistical」不是單純指「統計的」，而是帶有「由多數人數量決定」的語境，強調理性或正常不應只靠票數判定。
generatedExample: Her choice was practical, not statistical, because she cared about quality over numbers.

explanationZhTw: 「profound wisdom」指深刻、很有洞見的智慧。
generatedExample: His quiet comment carried a profound wisdom that stayed with me.

explanationZhTw: 「sensation」在這裡是「感覺、體感」的意思，指他醒來時主觀上覺得自己睡了很久。
generatedExample: I had a strange sensation that someone was watching me.

explanationZhTw: 「glance」是「瞥一眼、匆匆看一下」，表示他只是快速看了鐘一眼就知道時間。
generatedExample: She took a quick glance at the message and kept walking.

explanationZhTw: 「old-fashioned」在這裡是「老式的、過時風格的」，用來形容那個鐘的外觀或設計。
generatedExample: My grandfather still uses an old-fashioned radio.

explanationZhTw: 「dozing」是「打盹、半睡半醒」，比正式的睡眠更輕，表示他只是小睡了一會兒。
generatedExample: I was dozing on the sofa when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 「struck up」這裡是片語動詞，意思是「開始響起、開始唱起來」。
generatedExample: The band struck up as soon as the guests arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fancy` 不是「想像」的普通意思，而是指一時的愛慕、迷戀或短暫的情愫。
generatedExample: At first I thought it was only a fancy, but it turned into real love.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `stirred` 表示「激起、喚起」情感或想法，不是單純的「攪動」。
generatedExample: Her kind words stirred memories I had almost forgotten.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「偷走我的心、讓我深深著迷／墜入愛河」。這裡帶有方言拼寫。
generatedExample: The way she smiled at me had stolen my heart away.

explanationZhTw: 「drivelling」在這裡不是字面上的「流口水」，而是帶有貶義，表示這首歌很低俗、愚蠢或令人厭煩。
generatedExample: The reviewer dismissed the movie as drivelling nonsense.

explanationZhTw: 「all over the place」是慣用語，表示「到處、隨處都聽得到／看得到」。
generatedExample: After the launch, their posters were all over the place.

explanationZhTw: 「outlived」在這裡表示「比……更長久存在；存活／流行得比……久」。語意重點不是單純的「活得更久」，而是「在時間上比另一首歌更持久」。
generatedExample: This slogan outlived the campaign that created it.

explanationZhTw: 「luxuriously」表示「舒舒服服地、帶著享受感地」，用來描述 Julia 伸展身體時那種慵懶愜意的感覺。
generatedExample: He sank luxuriously into the armchair after a long day.

explanationZhTw: hungry 表示「餓的、感到飢餓」；這是很常見的基礎字彙。
generatedExample: After the long walk, I was hungry.

explanationZhTw: gone out 在這裡指火或爐火「熄滅了」，不是「出去了」。
generatedExample: The candle went out when the wind blew.

explanationZhTw: picked ... up 在這裡是「把東西拿起來、舉起來」的意思。
generatedExample: She picked up the box and moved it to the table.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 oil 指爐子使用的燃料油，不是一般烹調用的油。
generatedExample: The heater stopped because it had no oil.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `get some from` 是口語用法，意思是「從……那裡弄到／拿到一些東西」，重點在取得來源，不是單純字面上的「得到一些」。
generatedExample: We can get some from the shop across the street.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `old` 不是在說查林頓真的年紀很大，而是英式口語中帶點熟稔、親暱的稱呼方式，相當於「老查林頓」或「那位查林頓先生」。
generatedExample: I saw old Mr. Hayes at the station this morning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 funny 不是「好笑」，而是「奇怪、出人意料」；整個片語表示「奇怪的是／有趣的是」。
generatedExample: The funny thing is that everyone expected rain, but the sky stayed clear.

explanationZhTw: make sure 表示「確定、確認、務必」；這是很常見的基礎表達。
generatedExample: Please make sure the door is locked before you leave.

explanationZhTw: put on 表示「穿上（衣服）」。這裡是把衣服穿回去。
generatedExample: I put my jacket on because it started to rain.

explanationZhTw: get + 形容詞常表示「變得……」；這裡是「變冷了」。
generatedExample: It got colder after sunset.

explanationZhTw: 「got up」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「起床、起身」。
generatedExample: I got up early to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: 「indefatigable」是形容詞，表示「不知疲倦的、持續不停的」。這裡用來形容聲音一直沒有停下來，屬於較不常見、帶文學感的用法。
generatedExample: She was an indefatigable worker who never gave up.

explanationZhTw: 「sang on」在這裡表示「繼續唱下去」，強調動作持續不斷。
generatedExample: The choir sang on until the audience left.

explanationZhTw: 這是諺語／慣用語，意思是時間會慢慢沖淡痛苦、讓問題逐漸好轉。
generatedExample: After a few months, he felt that time heals all things and the pain became easier to bear.

explanationZhTw: heartstrings 指「內心深處的情感」；這裡表示這些回憶強烈觸動了我的情緒。
generatedExample: The child’s letter tugged at my heartstrings and made me cry.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `strolled across to` 表示「漫不經心地走到……去」，語氣比單純 `went to` 更悠閒。
generatedExample: She strolled across to the desk and picked up the note.

explanationZhTw: `to and fro` 是固定用法，意思是「來來回回、反覆地」。
generatedExample: The cat paced to and fro in front of the door.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，指她不停彎腰、起身、做事，像瓶塞一樣反覆「塞上、打開」。
generatedExample: He kept corking and uncorking himself as he hurried around the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: `pegging out` 在這裡是「用衣夾把衣物晾在繩子上」。
generatedExample: She spent the afternoon pegging out the shirts in the garden.

explanationZhTw: `take in washing` 是慣用說法，意思是「替別人洗衣服賺錢」。
generatedExample: My grandmother used to take in washing to earn extra money.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `come across` 不是「偶然遇到」，而是「走過來、移到另一邊」。
generatedExample: After the meeting, she came across to my side of the room.

explanationZhTw: `blown up to monstrous dimensions` 是誇張比喻，表示「被撐大到可怕的程度」。
generatedExample: The rumors blew the mistake up to monstrous dimensions.

explanationZhTw: `coarse in the grain` 是形容質地很粗糙，像木頭紋理一樣不細緻。
generatedExample: The fabric was coarse in the grain and uncomfortable to wear.

explanationZhTw: `held inferior to` 表示「被認為比……差、被視為次等」。
generatedExample: This version should not be held inferior to the original.

explanationZhTw: 「beautiful」指「美麗的、漂亮的」，用來形容人、事物或景色很吸引人。
generatedExample: The painting is beautiful and full of light.

explanationZhTw: 「murmured」指「低聲說、喃喃地說」，通常表示說話音量很小，帶點溫柔、含糊或私下的感覺。
generatedExample: He murmured an apology before leaving the room.

explanationZhTw: 「metre」是長度單位「公尺」；這裡用來表示臀部的寬度大約有一公尺。
generatedExample: The table is two metres long.

explanationZhTw: 「across the hips」在這裡不是「穿過臀部」，而是指「以臀部那一段來量寬度」，也就是臀部很寬。
generatedExample: The dress is a bit tight across the hips.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `style` 不是單指「風格」，而是偏向「類型、樣式」的意思；整句是在說「那是她那一類的美」。
generatedExample: Her style of cooking is simple but elegant.

explanationZhTw: 形容身體柔軟、靈活；這裡用來描寫腰部的柔韌感。
generatedExample: The dancer's movements looked supple and effortless.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是軍事上的「側翼」，而是指身體的側面。
generatedExample: A cold wind hit my left flank as I crossed the hill.

explanationZhTw: 指靠人與人之間口頭轉述、口耳相傳，沒有書面記錄。
generatedExample: I heard about the concert by word of mouth.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示把訊息、秘密或東西「傳遞／轉告」給別人。
generatedExample: Please pass on my thanks to your team.

explanationZhTw: 比喻短暫的盛放或全盛期，暗示美貌只維持很短時間。
generatedExample: Her momentary flowering as a singer came and went in one summer.

explanationZhTw: 表示帶有敬意、近乎崇敬的尊重。
generatedExample: He spoke with reverence about his old teacher.

explanationZhTw: 形容幾乎沒有盡頭、長得令人厭煩。
generatedExample: The meeting felt interminable after two hours.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「累積、儲存」的意思，強調把力量慢慢聚集起來。
generatedExample: She is storing up energy for the race.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「在於、存在於」，不是單純的躺著。
generatedExample: The problem lay in the rushed schedule.

explanationZhTw: 表示對某人來說很陌生、格格不入。
generatedExample: That style of music is alien to me.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「至少、起碼」，語氣在這裡帶有最低限度的保證。
generatedExample: At the least, we should call to confirm.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示在各種不利條件下仍然存活、成功或持續下去。
generatedExample: Against all the odds, the startup survived its first year.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「傳遞下去」，可指把生命力或訊息一代接一代延續。
generatedExample: He kept passing on the same story from one friend to another.

explanationZhTw: 指生命力、活力、能量。
generatedExample: A good night's sleep restored my vitality.

explanationZhTw: 「remember」是動詞，表示「記得、想起來」。這裡是在回想過去的經歷。
generatedExample: I remember the first time we met at school.

explanationZhTw: 「thrush」在這裡不是疾病，而是指「畫眉鳥」這類會唱歌的小鳥。
generatedExample: A thrush was singing in the tree outside my window.

explanationZhTw: 「the wood」在這裡是「樹林、林地」的意思，不是「木頭」；整個片語指「樹林邊緣」。
generatedExample: We stopped at the edge of the wood before it got dark.

explanationZhTw: 「to please himself」在這裡不是指「取悅自己」的字面動作，而是指「只是為了自己的興致／自得其樂地唱，不是為了別人」。
generatedExample: She wasn't performing for an audience; she was doing it to please herself.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「just」表示「只是、單純地」，用來強調他沒有別的目的。
generatedExample: I wasn't arguing; I was just asking a question.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「無產階級／勞工階層」，帶有政治與階級色彩，並不是一般口語中的「人群」意思。
generatedExample: The proles filled the square after work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「邊境、國界」，不是「最前沿」或抽象的前線。
generatedExample: The old frontiers between the two countries are still visible on maps.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「怪物」，而是形容被勞作與生育扭曲得可怕、巨大或令人不安。
generatedExample: The factory made the old building look monstrous at night.

explanationZhTw: 表示「辛苦地工作、埋頭勞作」，語氣比一般的 work 更強，帶出勞累與持續不斷的辛苦。
generatedExample: She spent the whole day toiling in the field.

explanationZhTw: 文學用語，通常指「腰腹、胯部」，也可暗示繁衍後代的力量或血統來源。
generatedExample: The epic praised the king's loins as the source of a great line of rulers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「維持、保存、守住」某種狀態或精神，不是單純的「救活」。
generatedExample: They kept the tradition alive by teaching it to children.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「維持、保存、守住」某種狀態或精神，不是單純的「救活」。
generatedExample: They kept the tradition alive by teaching it to children.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「傳遞、轉告、傳授」；在這裡是把秘密教義傳下去。
generatedExample: She passed on the lesson her teacher gave her.

explanationZhTw: 指一套被堅持、傳承的信念或教義；這裡帶有政治/思想體系的意味。
generatedExample: The group defended its doctrine in every debate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `the dead` 不是單純形容「死掉的人」的字面說法，而是帶有文學與語境色彩的名詞片語，指「已經注定會被消滅／等同於死去的人」。
generatedExample: After the betrayal, they felt like the dead, with no future left to protect.

explanationZhTw: `echoed` 在這裡不是指「回音」名詞，而是動詞，表示把前一句話重複說出來。
generatedExample: The crowd echoed the speaker's final words.

explanationZhTw: `dutifully` 表示「順從地、盡責地」，帶有照著要求去做、沒有反抗的語氣。
generatedExample: He dutifully completed the form before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「iron voice」不是字面上的「鐵製聲音」，而是比喻說話聲冷硬、沒有感情，帶有威嚴或威脅感。
generatedExample: She answered in an iron voice that made everyone stop talking.

explanationZhTw: 突然分開、猛地拉開距離；這裡描述兩人因驚訝或緊張而迅速分離。
generatedExample: When the door opened, the two children sprang apart and pretended they were not arguing.

explanationZhTw: 內臟；此處是文學性用法，表示他感到全身深處都像結冰一樣，強調極度驚恐。
generatedExample: At the bad news, her entrails seemed to twist with fear.

explanationZhTw: 虹膜；這是眼睛的解剖學名稱，指眼球有色的部分。
generatedExample: The doctor examined the irises of both eyes under a bright light.

explanationZhTw: 變成帶奶白感的淡黃色；這裡是描述臉色蒼白、失去血色的隱喻性說法。
generatedExample: After waiting in the cold for hours, his skin turned a milky yellow.

explanationZhTw: 格外顯眼、突兀地凸顯出來；此處表示腮紅在蒼白的臉上看起來非常刺眼。
generatedExample: Against the white wall, the red sign stood out sharply.

explanationZhTw: 重複說；再次說出同樣的話。這裡是常見動詞用法，屬於基礎字彙。
generatedExample: She repeated the question because I did not hear her.

explanationZhTw: 「iron voice」不是字面上的「鐵的聲音」，而是指聲音冷硬、毫無感情、帶有威嚴或壓迫感。
generatedExample: The manager spoke in an iron voice, and nobody interrupted.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `breathed` 不是字面上的「呼吸」，而是文學用法，表示「低聲說；輕聲吐出」。
generatedExample: She breathed the answer so no one else could hear it.

explanationZhTw: `remain` 表示「待在原地、繼續保持」，比單純的 `stay` 更正式，這裡是在下命令，要人不要移動。
generatedExample: Please remain seated until the plane stops.

explanationZhTw: `movement` 指「動作、移動」。在這裡是命令式用法，意思是「不要做任何動作」。
generatedExample: The doctor asked the patient to avoid sudden movement.

explanationZhTw: `ordered` 在這裡不是「已訂購」，而是「被命令、被下令」。這是上下文中的特殊意思。
generatedExample: You must leave the building when you are ordered to do so.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `except` 表示「除了；只能」, 用來強調他們沒有其他選擇，只能站著互看。
generatedExample: We could do nothing except wait for the storm to pass.

explanationZhTw: `too late` 在這裡表示「太遲了」, 指錯過了逃跑或補救的時機。
generatedExample: If we leave now, it is not too late to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: `the iron voice from the wall` 是帶有比喻的說法，指牆上的電幕傳出的命令聲，語氣冷硬、不可違抗。
generatedExample: The iron voice from the speaker told everyone to stay in their seats.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `uncovering` 不是單純「覆蓋」的相反，而是「露出、顯現出來」的意思。
generatedExample: The wind blew away the cloth, uncovering the statue underneath.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「看見、注意到」的意思，表示對方現在能用視覺發現我們。
generatedExample: They can see the house from the hill.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「很顯眼」，而是命令他們「站到中間來」；屬於依上下文才看得出來的用法。
generatedExample: Please stand out from the wall and wait near the door.

explanationZhTw: 指「中央、正中間」的位置。
generatedExample: The chair is in the middle of the room.

explanationZhTw: 表示「背靠背、背對背」站著。
generatedExample: The two students stood back to back during the game.

explanationZhTw: 意為「緊握、扣住、交叉扣緊」，比一般的 hold 更正式。
generatedExample: She clasped the necklace in her hands.

explanationZhTw: 表示位置在「後面、背後」。
generatedExample: He stood behind his friend in line.

explanationZhTw: 指「頭部」；這裡是複數，因為命令對多個人說。
generatedExample: Keep your heads up and walk forward.

explanationZhTw: 指「碰觸、接觸」；在這裡是否定命令。
generatedExample: Do not touch the wires with wet hands.

explanationZhTw: 表示「彼此、互相」，用來說明多人之間的相互動作。
generatedExample: The children were told to help one another.

explanationZhTw: 「chattering」在這裡指牙齒因寒冷、恐懼或緊張而不停打顫、互相撞擊。
generatedExample: My teeth were chattering after I stepped out into the snow.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，表示「他無法控制」；強調身體反應不受意志支配。
generatedExample: Her hands were shaking beyond her control.

explanationZhTw: 「trampling」在這裡不是單純「踩踏」，而是形容許多人或重物走動時發出的沉重腳步聲。
generatedExample: We heard trampling boots in the hallway before the door opened.

explanationZhTw: 「dragged」表示被拖著移動，通常是費力、摩擦地往前拉。
generatedExample: He dragged the chair across the floor.

explanationZhTw: 「abruptly」表示突然、毫無預警地。
generatedExample: The music stopped abruptly when the power went out.

explanationZhTw: 「clang」是金屬撞擊時發出的響亮刺耳聲。
generatedExample: A loud clang echoed through the empty workshop.

explanationZhTw: 「flung」是「用力丟、甩出去」的意思，這裡描寫物體被粗暴地扔過去。
generatedExample: Someone flung the door open and ran inside.

explanationZhTw: 「surrounded」在這裡是被動語態，意思是「被包圍、四周圍住」。
generatedExample: The village is surrounded by high walls.

explanationZhTw: `surrounded` 在這裡表示「被包圍、四周都有東西圍住」。這裡是被動語態，常用來描述人、建築或地點被一圈事物圍繞。
generatedExample: The building was surrounded by police after the alarm went off.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「彈跳」的字面意思，而是指把牙齒用力合上，通常帶有不耐煩、惱怒或緊張的語氣。
generatedExample: She snapped her teeth together when she heard the bad news.

explanationZhTw: 表示「既然沒有更好的選擇，不如就……／乾脆……」，語氣帶點無奈。
generatedExample: We may as well leave now since the rain is getting heavier.

explanationZhTw: 「may as well + 動詞原形」表示「不妨、倒不如」，常用來說某個做法和另一個做法差不多，帶點無奈或順勢而為的語氣。
generatedExample: Since the shop is closed, we may as well go home now.

explanationZhTw: 「good-bye」就是「再見、告別」。這裡表示某件事即將結束，帶有結束關係或機會的語感。
generatedExample: If we miss the last train, we may as well say good-bye to our plans for tonight.

explanationZhTw: 「struck in」在這裡不是字面上的「打進去」，而是指「插話、突然介入說話」。這是比較口語的用法。
generatedExample: Before I could finish, she struck in with a question.

explanationZhTw: 「by the way」是插入話題時常用的說法，意思是「順便一提、對了」。它用來切換到另一個補充點。
generatedExample: By the way, did you ever send that email?

explanationZhTw: 「while we are on the subject」意思是「既然正在談這個話題／趁著提到這件事」。這裡屬於依上下文才能完全理解的話題轉接表達。
generatedExample: While we are on the subject, we should also discuss next week's schedule.

explanationZhTw: 「crash on to」表示重重地撞上、掉到某處；這裡是指某物突然很大力地落到床上。
generatedExample: A heavy box crashed on to the floor in the hallway.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「head」不是「頭」，而是指梯子的頂端、前端。
generatedExample: The head of the nail was bent after the hammer struck it.

explanationZhTw: 「thrust through」表示用力插進、塞進或伸穿過去，強調動作很猛。
generatedExample: He thrust the note through the gap in the door.

explanationZhTw: 「burst in」這裡指把某物撞破、弄裂；不是「闖入」的意思。
generatedExample: The old pipe burst in the cold weather.

explanationZhTw: 「stampede」原本常指一群動物或人群驚慌奔跑；這裡借來形容一陣急促、混亂的腳步聲。
generatedExample: A stampede of shoppers rushed toward the exit when the alarm sounded.

explanationZhTw: 「solid」在這裡不是「固體的」，而是指身材結實、壯碩、看起來很有壓迫感。
generatedExample: The guard was a solid man with broad shoulders and a deep voice.

explanationZhTw: 「iron-shod」表示用鐵包著、鐵底的；這裡形容靴子很硬、很重。
generatedExample: The horse wore iron-shod hooves for extra protection.

explanationZhTw: 「truncheons」是警棍、短棍，常由警察或執法人員攜帶。
generatedExample: The officer held a truncheon at his side as he walked the corridor.

explanationZhTw: 「顫抖、發抖」；這裡指 Winston 已經不再因恐懼而發抖。
generatedExample: His hands were trembling in the cold.

explanationZhTw: 「保持不動、不要亂動」；句中兩次出現，都是強調只能盡量靜止不動。
generatedExample: Keep still while I tie your shoe.

explanationZhTw: 「保持不動、不要亂動」；句中兩次出現，都是強調只能盡量靜止不動。
generatedExample: Keep still while I tie your shoe.

explanationZhTw: 「藉口、理由」；這裡是指不要讓對方找到打人的理由。
generatedExample: She made an excuse for being late.

explanationZhTw: 「下頜；下垂的臉頰肉」；這裡用來描寫那個男人粗壯、兇硬的臉部輪廓。
generatedExample: The bulldog's jowl hung low on both sides of its mouth.

explanationZhTw: 「警棍」；指警察或守衛使用的棍棒。
generatedExample: The officer carried a truncheon on his belt.

explanationZhTw: 「若有所思地、沉思地」；描述他拿著警棍時慢慢思考的樣子。
generatedExample: He stared at the floor meditatively.

explanationZhTw: 「食指」；也就是緊鄰拇指的那根手指。
generatedExample: He pointed to the map with his forefinger.

explanationZhTw: 「赤裸感；毫無遮掩的狀態」；這裡不只是字面上的裸體，也帶有完全暴露、沒有防護的感覺。
generatedExample: She felt a sudden nakedness when the lights came on.

explanationZhTw: 「暴露的、無遮掩的」；在這裡也隱含「毫無防備、很脆弱」的意思。
generatedExample: The village was exposed to strong winds.

explanationZhTw: 「難以忍受的」；表示這種感受強烈到幾乎受不了。
generatedExample: The heat was unbearable in the tiny room.

explanationZhTw: 「伸出、突出」；這裡指他把舌尖伸出來。
generatedExample: A nail protruded from the wooden board.

explanationZhTw: 「成碎片、四分五裂」；表示東西被完全打碎了。
generatedExample: The vase fell and broke to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 「太陽神經叢」；這裡指腹部中央靠上的部位，受到重擊會非常痛。
generatedExample: He took a hard punch to the solar plexus and bent over तुरंत.

explanationZhTw: 猛烈地扭動、掙扎，通常表示身體失控地動來動去。
generatedExample: The fish kept thrashing about in the bucket.

explanationZhTw: 喘不過氣、努力維持呼吸；強調呼吸非常困難。
generatedExample: After running up the stairs, I was fighting for breath.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「暴怒的」，而是指臉色發青、發紫或灰暗，常見於受傷或缺氧。
generatedExample: Her livid face showed that she was badly injured.

explanationZhTw: 把她用力抬起、舉起；hoist 常帶有「費力往上抬」的意思。
generatedExample: They hoisted the heavy box up onto the truck.

explanationZhTw: 像麻袋一樣；形容人軟垮垮、毫無力氣，像物件被搬走。
generatedExample: He collapsed onto the bed like a sack.

explanationZhTw: 瞥見；只看到很短暫的一眼。
generatedExample: I caught a glimpse of the actor before he disappeared.

explanationZhTw: 扭曲變形；常用來形容因痛苦、恐懼或強烈情緒而變得難看的表情或姿勢。
generatedExample: Pain contorted his face for a moment.

explanationZhTw: idiom。意思是「一動不動、完全靜止」。
generatedExample: The guards stood dead still when the alarm went off.

explanationZhTw: idiom。意思是「自然而然地發生或出現，不是刻意促成的」。
generatedExample: The door opened of its own accord in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是語境義。flit 原本常指「快速飛掠、輕快移動」，這裡指念頭在腦中一閃而過。
generatedExample: A strange memory flitted through his mind during the speech.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是語境義。got 不是「得到」，而是「抓到、逮到、抓走」。
generatedExample: I hope they didn't get the messenger before he escaped.

explanationZhTw: term。意思是「排尿、小便」，比日常說法更正式或醫學化。
generatedExample: The doctor asked whether the patient could urinate normally.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 faint 是「輕微的、微弱的」，faint surprise 指「些微的驚訝」。
generatedExample: She showed faint surprise when the results were announced.

explanationZhTw: term。指「壁爐架上方的台面／壁爐架」，常放時鐘、相框或擺飾。
generatedExample: There was a candle and a photo on the mantelpiece.

explanationZhTw: idiom。意思是「一覺睡了很久，睡過了整個時段」；這裡指睡得連日期／時鐘循環都弄錯了。
generatedExample: After the long trip, I slept the clock round and woke up at noon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是語境義。pursue 不只是「追趕」，而是「繼續深入思考、繼續追查某個想法」。
generatedExample: She decided not to pursue the idea further after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 passage 指「走廊、通道」，不是「文章段落」或其他常見意思；需要靠上下文判斷。
generatedExample: The hotel passage was too narrow for the luggage cart.

explanationZhTw: came into 這裡表示「走進、進入」某個地方。
generatedExample: She came into the office after lunch.

explanationZhTw: demeanour 指一個人的舉止、神情或態度。
generatedExample: Her calm demeanour made the interview easier.

explanationZhTw: subdued 在這裡指「壓抑的、克制的、不張揚的」，語氣比一般的 quiet 更正式。
generatedExample: The celebration felt subdued after the bad news.

explanationZhTw: His eye fell on 是慣用說法，表示「他的目光落在／他注意到」某物。
generatedExample: My eye fell on an old photo in the drawer.

explanationZhTw: fragments 指「碎片、殘片」，通常是被打碎後留下的小部分。
generatedExample: We found fragments of glass on the floor.

explanationZhTw: 「pick up」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「撿起、收拾」。整句是在命令對方把那些碎片/碎塊撿起來。
generatedExample: Please pick up those books from the floor before you leave.

explanationZhTw: obey 是「服從、照做」；這裡指那個人立刻照命令行事。
generatedExample: She obeyed the rule without arguing.

explanationZhTw: cockney accent 指倫敦東區口音；這裡表示他的原本口音不見了。
generatedExample: Her cockney accent made her easy to recognize.

explanationZhTw: wearing 表示「穿著／戴著」。
generatedExample: He was wearing a blue coat.

explanationZhTw: identity 是「身分、身份」；這裡指確認他是不是同一個人。
generatedExample: The guard checked my identity at the door.

explanationZhTw: recognizable 指「還能辨認出來的」。
generatedExample: After the haircut, he was still recognizable.

explanationZhTw: straightened 在這裡是「挺直、變得筆直」，不是單純「修正」。
generatedExample: She straightened her back before speaking.

explanationZhTw: undergone 在這裡是「經歷、受到」；整句表示外表雖只小變動，卻造成徹底改變。
generatedExample: The building has undergone major repairs.

explanationZhTw: altered 這裡是「改變、變樣」，偏書面語。
generatedExample: The plan was altered at the last minute.

explanationZhTw: alert 這裡不是動詞，而是形容人「警覺、機警」。
generatedExample: The dog stayed alert all night.

explanationZhTw: occur to someone 表示「突然想到」；這裡是 Winston 突然意識到。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I left my keys at home.

explanationZhTw: member 是「成員」；這裡指某個組織裡的一員。
generatedExample: She is a member of the school club.

explanationZhTw: Thought Police 是小說中的專有名詞，指監視思想、打壓異見的秘密警察。
generatedExample: The novel presents the Thought Police as a symbol of total control.
